## 2. Anatomy

```text
DateTimePicker (en-US, closed, Minute granularity, H12)
└── Root                                data-ars-scope="date-time-picker"  data-ars-state="idle"
    ├── Label                           <label for="{id}-control">
    ├── Control                         role="group"  aria-labelledby="{id}-label"
    │   ├── DateSegmentGroup            role="group"  aria-label="Date"
    │   │   ├── Segment (Month)         role="spinbutton"  aria-valuemin="1"  aria-valuemax="12"
    │   │   ├── Literal "/"             aria-hidden="true"
    │   │   ├── Segment (Day)           role="spinbutton"  aria-valuemin="1"  aria-valuemax="31"
    │   │   ├── Literal "/"             aria-hidden="true"
    │   │   └── Segment (Year)          role="spinbutton"  aria-valuemin="1"  aria-valuemax="9999"
    │   ├── Separator                   aria-hidden="true"  (visual: ", " or " ")
    │   ├── TimeSegmentGroup            role="group"  aria-label="Time"
    │   │   ├── Segment (Hour)          role="spinbutton"  aria-valuemin="1"  aria-valuemax="12"
    │   │   ├── Literal ":"             aria-hidden="true"
    │   │   ├── Segment (Minute)        role="spinbutton"  aria-valuemin="0"  aria-valuemax="59"
    │   │   ├── Literal "\u{00A0}"      aria-hidden="true"
    │   │   └── Segment (DayPeriod)     role="spinbutton"  aria-label="AM/PM"
    │   ├── ClearTrigger                aria-label="Clear date and time"  [hidden when empty]
    │   └── Trigger                     aria-label="Open date and time picker"  aria-expanded="false"
    ├── Positioner                      [hidden when closed]
    │   └── Content                     role="dialog"
    │       └── <Calendar />            Embedded Calendar component
    ├── Description                     optional help text
    ├── ErrorMessage                    role="alert"  aria-live="polite"
    └── HiddenInput                     type="hidden"  name="..."  value="2024-03-15T14:30:00"
```

| Part               | HTML Element            | Required | Key Attributes                                               |
| ------------------ | ----------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Root`             | `<div>`                 | yes      | `data-ars-scope`, `data-ars-state`                           |
| `Label`            | `<label>`               | yes      | `for` pointing to Control                                    |
| `Control`          | `<div>`                 | yes      | `role="group"`, `aria-labelledby`                            |
| `DateSegmentGroup` | `<div>`                 | yes      | `role="group"`, `aria-label="Date"`                          |
| `TimeSegmentGroup` | `<div>`                 | yes      | `role="group"`, `aria-label="Time"`                          |
| `Segment`          | `<div>`                 | yes      | `role="spinbutton"`, `tabindex="0"`, `aria-valuenow/min/max` |
| `Literal`          | `<span>`                | yes      | `aria-hidden="true"`                                         |
| `Separator`        | `<span>`                | yes      | `aria-hidden="true"`                                         |
| `Trigger`          | `<button>`              | yes      | `aria-label`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`               |
| `ClearTrigger`     | `<button>`              | optional | `aria-label`, hidden when no value                           |
| `Positioner`       | `<div>`                 | yes      | Hidden when popover closed                                   |
| `Content`          | `<div role="dialog">`   | yes      | Contains embedded Calendar                                   |
| `Description`      | `<div>`                 | optional | Help text                                                    |
| `ErrorMessage`     | `<div role="alert">`    | optional | Validation error; `aria-live="polite"`                       |
| `HiddenInput`      | `<input type="hidden">` | yes      | ISO 8601 datetime string for form submission                 |

## 3. Accessibility

### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties

| Element            | Role/Attribute          | Details                                                                                |
| ------------------ | ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Control`          | `role="group"`          | Groups all segments; `aria-labelledby` points to Label                                 |
| `DateSegmentGroup` | `role="group"`          | `aria-label="Date"`; groups date segments                                              |
| `TimeSegmentGroup` | `role="group"`          | `aria-label="Time"`; groups time segments                                              |
| `Segment`          | `role="spinbutton"`     | `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuetext`, `aria-label`      |
| `Trigger`          | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Open date and time picker"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`             |
| `ClearTrigger`     | `<button>`              | `aria-label="Clear date and time"`                                                     |
| `Content`          | `role="dialog"`         | Contains the Calendar; announced when opened                                           |
| `ErrorMessage`     | `role="alert"`          | `aria-live="polite"`; announced immediately on render                                  |
| `Literal`          | `<span>`                | `aria-hidden="true"`; decorative separators                                            |
| `Separator`        | `<span>`                | `aria-hidden="true"`; decorative separator between date and time                       |
| `HiddenInput`      | `<input type="hidden">` | Carries the ISO 8601 value; `aria-describedby` chains Description and ErrorMessage IDs |

When `disabled=true`: all segments receive `aria-disabled="true"`, trigger and clear buttons are disabled.
When `readonly=true`: all segments receive `aria-readonly="true"`, trigger and clear buttons are disabled.
When `required=true`: the DateSegmentGroup receives `aria-required="true"`.
When `invalid=true`: the DateSegmentGroup receives `aria-invalid="true"`, and the ErrorMessage part is rendered.

### 3.2 Keyboard Interaction

| Key                    | Context                            | Action                                                     |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ArrowUp`              | Segment focused                    | Increment the focused segment value by 1, wrapping         |
| `ArrowDown`            | Segment focused                    | Decrement the focused segment value by 1, wrapping         |
| `ArrowLeft`            | Segment focused                    | Move focus to the previous editable segment (date or time) |
| `ArrowRight`           | Segment focused                    | Move focus to the next editable segment (date or time)     |
| `Tab`                  | Last date segment focused          | Move focus to the first time segment                       |
| `Shift+Tab`            | First time segment focused         | Move focus to the last date segment                        |
| `Tab`                  | Last time segment focused          | Move focus to the trigger button                           |
| `0`-`9`                | Numeric segment focused            | Type-ahead entry; auto-advances to next segment when full  |
| `a` / `p`              | DayPeriod segment focused          | Set AM / PM                                                |
| `Backspace` / `Delete` | Segment focused                    | Clear the focused segment value                            |
| `Enter` / `Space`      | Trigger focused                    | Toggle the popover open/closed                             |
| `ArrowDown`            | Trigger focused or segment focused | Open the calendar popover (Alt+ArrowDown at adapter level) |
| `Escape`               | Popover open                       | Close the popover; return focus to the trigger             |

> RTL: `ArrowLeft` and `ArrowRight` swap direction for segment navigation per `03-accessibility.md` section 4.1.

### 3.3 Focus Management

1. User tabs to the first date segment or the trigger button.
2. Arrow keys navigate between segments within the date and time groups. Navigation crosses the date-to-time boundary seamlessly.
3. Clicking the trigger or pressing Enter/Space on it opens the calendar popover.
4. When the popover opens, focus moves into the Calendar grid (first selected date, or today).
5. Selecting a date in the calendar closes the popover and moves focus to the first time segment, so the user can confirm or adjust the time.
6. Pressing Escape in the popover closes it and returns focus to the trigger.
7. Tab from inside the calendar closes the popover and moves focus to the next focusable element after the DateTimePicker.
8. When focus leaves the component entirely (`FocusOut`), any pending type-ahead buffer is committed and the popover closes.

All user-visible text is provided via the `Messages` struct. Individual segment labels (e.g., "Year", "Hour", "AM/PM") are inherited from the `DateSegmentKind::aria_label()` method defined in date-field's segment types. Date segment order and separators are locale-dependent (see `Machine::build_date_segments()`). Time segment display uses the locale's preferred hour cycle when `hour_cycle` is `None`.

## 5. Form Integration

The DateTimePicker participates in HTML form submission via a hidden `<input type="hidden">` element rendered by the `HiddenInput` part. The hidden input carries the combined date-time value in ISO 8601 format.

- **ISO value**: The hidden input's `value` is always `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` (e.g., `2024-03-15T14:30:00`), regardless of the display format or locale used in the visible segments.
- **`name` prop**: When set, the hidden input includes `name="{value}"` so the date-time is submitted with the form under that key.
- **Required validation**: When `required=true`, the DateSegmentGroup carries `aria-required="true"`. Form validation treats an empty value (no date-time selected) as invalid.
- **Reset**: On form reset, the adapter restores the initial value (the `default_value` from Props or `None`) and rebuilds all segments accordingly.
- **`aria-describedby`**: The Control group's `aria-describedby` chains the Description and ErrorMessage part IDs so assistive technology announces help text and validation errors in context.
- **Disabled/readonly propagation**: When the DateTimePicker is nested inside a form context that sets `disabled` or `readonly`, those values propagate per `07-forms.md` section 15.

## 6. Library Parity

> Compared against: Ark UI (`DatePicker`).

Ark UI does not have a dedicated DateTimePicker component. Its `DatePicker` supports time selection only indirectly (time segments are not part of its anatomy). ars-ui's DateTimePicker is a novel composite that unifies date segments, time segments, and a calendar popover into a single state machine, providing a more integrated experience than Ark UI's date-only picker.

### 6.1 Props

| Granularity      | `granularity` (TimeGranularity)     | --                       | ars-ui adds time granularity               |
| Hour cycle       | `hour_cycle`                        | --                       | ars-ui adds 12/24h support                 |

| Disabled         | `disabled`                          | `disabled`               | Equivalent                                 |
| Read-only        | `readonly`                          | `readOnly`               | Equivalent                                 |
| Required         | `required`                          | `required`               | Equivalent                                 |

| Trigger       | `Trigger`                      | `Trigger`      | Equivalent                       |
| Clear trigger | `ClearTrigger`                 | `ClearTrigger` | Equivalent                       |
| Positioner    | `Positioner`                   | `Positioner`   | Equivalent                       |
| Content       | `Content`                      | `Content`      | Equivalent                       |
| Description   | `Description`                  | --             | ars-ui form integration          |
| Error message | `ErrorMessage`                 | --             | ars-ui form integration          |
| Hidden input  | `HiddenInput`                  | --             | ars-ui form integration          |
| Literal       | `Literal`                      | --             | Separators between segments      |

**Gaps:** None. ars-ui has a richer anatomy.

### 6.3 Events

| Callback        | ars-ui                                       | Ark UI          | Notes                                  |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Value change    | `ValueCommit` / `SegmentChange`              | `onValueChange` | Equivalent                             |
| Open change     | `Open` / `Close`                             | `onOpenChange`  | Equivalent                             |
| Segment editing | `IncrementSegment`, `DecrementSegment`, etc. | --              | ars-ui has fine-grained segment events |

**Gaps:** None.

### 6.4 Features

| Feature                           | ars-ui | Ark UI |
| --------------------------------- | ------ | ------ |
| Date + time input                 | Yes    | No     |
| Calendar popover                  | Yes    | Yes    |
| Segmented editing (spinbutton)    | Yes    | No     |
| Type-ahead numeric entry          | Yes    | No     |
| AM/PM segment                     | Yes    | No     |
| Cross-boundary segment navigation | Yes    | No     |
| Hidden form input (ISO 8601)      | Yes    | No     |

**Gaps:** None. ars-ui is a superset.

### 6.5 Summary

- **Overall:** ars-ui exceeds Ark UI's capabilities since Ark UI has no dedicated date-time picker.
- **Divergences:** ars-ui combines date and time segments in a single machine with a unified calendar popover. Ark UI would require composing separate DatePicker and time input components.
- **Recommended additions:** None.

### 1. **DateSegment** struct

```rust
/// A single segment within a `DateField`.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct DateSegment {
    /// The kind of segment.
    pub kind: DateSegmentKind,

    /// Current numeric value, or `None` when the user has not entered it.
    pub value: Option<i32>,

    /// Minimum valid numeric value for editable segments.
    pub min: i32,

    /// Maximum valid numeric value for editable segments.
    pub max: i32,

    /// Formatted display text for the current value.
    pub text: String,

    /// Placeholder shown when the segment has no value.
    pub placeholder: String,

    /// Literal text for non-editable separator segments.
    pub literal: Option<String>,

    /// Whether this segment accepts keyboard input.
    pub is_editable: bool,
}
```

**Constructor Methods:**
- **`new_numeric(kind: DateSegmentKind, min: i32, max: i32, placeholder: &str) -> Self`** (lines 136-147)
- **`new_literal(ch: &str) -> Self`** (lines 151-162)

### 8. **Bindable<T>** type
**File:** `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-292/crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs`

**Lines 497-582:**
```rust
pub struct Bindable<T: BindableValue> {
    /// The externally controlled value, or `None` if uncontrolled.
    controlled: Option<T>,

    /// The internal value managed by the component.
    internal: T,
}

impl<T: BindableValue> Bindable<T> {
    /// Creates a controlled bindable owned by the parent.
    pub fn controlled(value: T) -> Self { ... }

    /// Creates an uncontrolled bindable managed by the component.
    pub const fn uncontrolled(default: T) -> Self { ... }

    /// Returns a reference to the current value.
    pub fn get(&self) -> &T { ... }

    /// Returns `true` if this value is controlled by the parent.
    pub const fn is_controlled(&self) -> bool { ... }

    /// Updates the internal value.
    pub fn set(&mut self, value: T) { ... }

    /// Pushes a new controlled value from the parent.
    pub fn sync_controlled(&mut self, value: Option<T>) { ... }

    /// Returns a mutable reference to the internal value.
    pub const fn get_mut_owned(&mut self) -> &mut T { ... }

    /// Returns the internal (pending) value, ignoring any controlled override.
    pub const fn pending(&self) -> &T { ... }
}
```

**Exact signatures:**
- **`controlled(value: T) -> Self`** (lines 511-516)
- **`uncontrolled(default: T) -> Self`** (const fn, lines 523-528)
- **`get(&self) -> &T`** (lines 534-536): Returns controlled if set, else internal
- **`set(&mut self, value: T)`** (lines 548-550)
- **`is_controlled(&self) -> bool`** (const fn, lines 540-542)
- **`sync_controlled(&mut self, value: Option<T>)`** (lines 555-557)
- **`get_mut_owned(&mut self) -> &mut T`** (const fn, lines 564-566)

### 9. Helper Functions
**File:** `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-292/crates/ars-components/src/date_time/time_field/mod.rs`

- **`digits_needed(max: u32) -> usize`** (const fn, lines 2014-2021):
  ```rust
  const fn digits_needed(max: u32) -> usize {
      match max {
          0..=9 => 1,
          10..=99 => 2,
          100..=999 => 3,
          _ => 4,
      }
  }
  ```

- **`format_time_segment(backend: &dyn IntlBackend, locale: &Locale, kind: DateSegmentKind, value: i32, force_leading_zeros: bool) -> String`** (lines 1973-1996)

- **`clamp_time(time: Time, min_value: Option<&Time>, max_value: Option<&Time>) -> Time`** (lines 1998-2012): Used for min/max validation

---

### 10. TimeField Type-Ahead & Segment Handling

**Key transitions from TimeField state machine:**

**TypeIntoSegment event handling** (lines 1405-1531):
```rust
fn type_into_segment(
    ctx: &Context,
    state: &State,
    kind: DateSegmentKind,
    ch: char,
) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Machine>>
```
- For numeric segments (Hour/Minute/Second), accumulates digits in `ctx.type_buffer`
- Uses `digits_needed(u32::try_from(segment.max).ok()?)` to detect auto-advance
- Auto-advances to next segment when buffer length >= max_digits OR `buffered * 10 > segment.max`
- Calls `maybe_publish(ctx)` after setting segment value

**commit_type_buffer / commit_buffer_for_kind** (lines 1533-1583):
```rust
fn commit_type_buffer(ctx: &mut Context)
fn commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx: &mut Context, kind: DateSegmentKind, timeout_fallback: bool)
```
- Validates buffer value is within segment min/max range
- Clears buffer after commit
- Calls `maybe_publish(ctx)` to potentially emit value if all segments complete

**maybe_publish** (lines 1616-1630):
```rust
fn maybe_publish(ctx: &mut Context) {
    if !ctx.is_complete() {
        return;
    }

    let Some(time) = ctx.assemble_time() else {
        return;
    };

    let clamped = clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref());

    apply_segments_from_time(ctx, clamped);

    ctx.value.set(Some(clamped));
}
```
- Only publishes if `is_complete()` (all editable segments have values)
- Calls `assemble_time()` to build validated Time from segments
- Clamps to min/max bounds
- Calls `ctx.value.set(Some(clamped))` to update the Bindable value

**Segment building** (lines 1753-1826):
```rust
fn build_segments(ctx: &Context) -> Vec<DateSegment>
```
- Creates hour segment: `DateSegment::new_numeric(DateSegmentKind::Hour, hour_min, hour_max, "hh")`
- Adds literal `:` between hour/minute/second
- For day period (H11/H12), adds non-editable day-period segment with min=0, max=1
- All segments initialized with values from current `ctx.value` if present

---

### 11. Context struct key fields
**File:** `/Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-292/crates/ars-components/src/date_time/time_field/mod.rs`

**Lines 361-410:**
```rust
pub struct Context {
    pub value: Bindable<Option<Time>>,    // Controlled or uncontrolled
    pub segments: Vec<DateSegment>,        // Display segments
    pub focused_segment: Option<DateSegmentKind>,
    pub type_buffer: String,               // Type-ahead accumulator
    pub is_composing: bool,                // IME composition flag
    pub locale: Locale,
    pub messages: Messages,
    pub intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>,
    pub granularity: TimeGranularity,      // Hour/Minute/Second
    pub hour_cycle: HourCycle,             // H11/H12/H23/H24
    pub disabled: bool,
    pub readonly: bool,
    pub min_value: Option<Time>,
    pub max_value: Option<Time>,
    pub ids: ComponentIds,
    pub force_leading_zeros: bool,
}
```

---

This comprehensive reference provides all exact definitions, file paths, line numbers, and method signatures needed to implement a DateTimePicker using the shared types and patterns established in the TimeField implementation.

```rust
pub struct Bindable<T: BindableValue> {
    /// The externally controlled value, or `None` if uncontrolled.
    controlled: Option<T>,

    /// The internal value managed by the component.
    internal: T,
}

impl<T: BindableValue> Bindable<T> {
    /// Creates a controlled bindable owned by the parent.
    pub fn controlled(value: T) -> Self { ... }

    /// Creates an uncontrolled bindable managed by the component.
    pub const fn uncontrolled(default: T) -> Self { ... }

    /// Returns a reference to the current value.
    pub fn get(&self) -> &T { ... }

    /// Returns `true` if this value is controlled by the parent.
    pub const fn is_controlled(&self) -> bool { ... }

    /// Updates the internal value.
    pub fn set(&mut self, value: T) { ... }

    /// Pushes a new controlled value from the parent.
    pub fn sync_controlled(&mut self, value: Option<T>) { ... }

    /// Returns a mutable reference to the internal value.
    pub const fn get_mut_owned(&mut self) -> &mut T { ... }

    /// Returns the internal (pending) value, ignoring any controlled override.
    pub const fn pending(&self) -> &T { ... }
}
```

### 10. TimeField Type-Ahead & Segment Handling

**Key transitions from TimeField state machine:**

**TypeIntoSegment event handling** (lines 1405-1531):
```rust
fn type_into_segment(
    ctx: &Context,
    state: &State,
    kind: DateSegmentKind,
    ch: char,
) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Machine>>
```
- For numeric segments (Hour/Minute/Second), accumulates digits in `ctx.type_buffer`
- Uses `digits_needed(u32::try_from(segment.max).ok()?)` to detect auto-advance
- Auto-advances to next segment when buffer length >= max_digits OR `buffered * 10 > segment.max`
- Calls `maybe_publish(ctx)` after setting segment value

**commit_type_buffer / commit_buffer_for_kind** (lines 1533-1583):
```rust
fn commit_type_buffer(ctx: &mut Context)
fn commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx: &mut Context, kind: DateSegmentKind, timeout_fallback: bool)
```
- Validates buffer value is within segment min/max range
- Clears buffer after commit
- Calls `maybe_publish(ctx)` to potentially emit value if all segments complete

**maybe_publish** (lines 1616-1630):
```rust
fn maybe_publish(ctx: &mut Context) {
    if !ctx.is_complete() {
        return;
    }

    let Some(time) = ctx.assemble_time() else {
        return;
    };

    let clamped = clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref());

    apply_segments_from_time(ctx, clamped);

    ctx.value.set(Some(clamped));
}
```
- Only publishes if `is_complete()` (all editable segments have values)
- Calls `assemble_time()` to build validated Time from segments
- Clamps to min/max bounds
- Calls `ctx.value.set(Some(clamped))` to update the Bindable value

**Segment building** (lines 1753-1826):
```rust
fn build_segments(ctx: &Context) -> Vec<DateSegment>
```
- Creates hour segment: `DateSegment::new_numeric(DateSegmentKind::Hour, hour_min, hour_max, "hh")`
- Adds literal `:` between hour/minute/second
- For day period (H11/H12), adds non-editable day-period segment with min=0, max=1
- All segments initialized with values from current `ctx.value` if present

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, ars_core::HasId)]
pub struct Props {
    pub id: String,
    pub value: Option<Option<CalendarDate>>,                    // ✓ Option<Option<CalendarDate>>

    pub show_week_numbers: bool,
    pub is_rtl: bool,
    pub visible_months: usize,
    pub page_behavior: PageBehavior,
    pub today: CalendarDate,
}

impl Default for Props {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            id: String::new(),
            value: None,
            default_value: None,
            selected_dates: None,
            default_selected_dates: SelectedDates::new(),
            selection_mode: SelectionMode::Single,
            max_selected: None,
            min: None,
            max: None,
            disabled: false,
            readonly: false,
            is_date_unavailable: None,
            first_day_of_week: None,
            show_week_numbers: false,
            is_rtl: false,
            visible_months: 1,
            page_behavior: PageBehavior::Visible,
            today: CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025, 1, 1)

}
```

887	}
888

890	    /// Creates a date-time from its date and time parts.
891	    #[must_use]
892	    pub const fn new(date: CalendarDate, time: Time) -> Self {
893	        Self { date, time }
894	    }
895	
896	    /// Returns the date portion.
897	    #[must_use]

899	        &self.date
900	    }
901	
902	    /// Returns the time portion.
903	    #[must_use]
904	    pub const fn time(&self) -> &Time {
905	        &self.time
906	    }
907	
908	    /// Adds a duration.
909	    ///
910	    /// # Errors
911	    ///
912	    /// Returns an error if the arithmetic result cannot be represented.
913	    pub fn add(&self, duration: DateTimeDuration) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
914	        let current = temporal_date_time_for(self)?;
915	
916	        let next = current
917	            .add(&duration.to_temporal()?, Some(Overflow::Constrain))
918	            .map_err(|error| CalendarError::Arithmetic(error.to_string()))?;
919	
920	        from_temporal_datetime(self.date.calendar, &next)
921	    }
922	
923	    /// Subtracts a duration.
924	    ///

927	    /// Returns an error if the arithmetic result cannot be represented.
928	    pub fn subtract(&self, duration: DateTimeDuration) -> Result<Self, CalendarError> {
929	        self.add(DateTimeDuration {
930	            date: DateDuration {
931	                years: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.date.years, "year")?,
932	                months: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.date.months, "month")?,
933	                weeks: checked_neg_i32_for_calendar(duration.date.weeks, "week")?,

content.text: I have complete ground truth. Before finalizing the plan, two genuine architectural decisions affect scope and the spec rewrite. The rest I can decide from established conventions.

questions.question: TimeField has private hour-cycle helpers (display_hour, display_hour_to_24, has_day_period, display_hour_range, is_pm) that DateTimePicker needs for its time segments. How should I share them?

options.label: Promote to shared module
options.description: Move the helpers into a shared date_time submodule (e.g. date_time::hour_cycle) and have both time_field and date_time_picker import them. DRY, single source of truth, but the PR also refactors time_field.

options.label: Duplicate in DateTimePicker
options.description: Copy the small const fns into date_time_picker. Keeps the PR scoped to the new component with no time_field churn, but duplicates hour-cycle math (drift risk).

questions.question: The spec's segment aria-labels say they're 'inherited from DateSegmentKind::aria_label()', but that method requires a date_field::Messages (covering year/month/day/hour/minute/second/day-period). DateTimePicker's own Messages struct doesn't carry per-segment labels. How should segment labels be sourced?

options.label: Reuse date_field::Messages
options.description: Store a date_field::Messages in Context (defaulted) as the segment-label source via DateSegmentKind::aria_label(). Localizable, no duplicated label set. Document this in spec §4.1.

options.label: Add segment fields to Messages
options.description: Add hour_label/minute_label/year_label/etc. fields directly onto DateTimePicker::Messages. More explicit but duplicates the date_field label surface and grows the public Messages API.

input.content:
# Task #292 — Implement DateTimePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #292 (Epic #224) asks for the framework-agnostic core of `DateTimePicker`: a
composite that unifies a **segmented date input** (like `DateField`), a **segmented time input**
(like `TimeField`), and a **calendar popover + trigger** (like `DatePicker`) into a single state
machine that owns both date and time segments and produces a combined `CalendarDateTime` value.
Dependencies #272 (TimeField) and #289 (DatePicker) are both merged. Scope is the agnostic
machine only — no Leptos/Dioxus adapters. Test tier: Unit (+ snapshots + spec-conformance).

**Important finding — the spec is heavily drifted.** `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`
§1.3/§1.7/§1.8/§4.1 was written against idealized pseudo-code that does **not** compile against
the real workspace API, while the sibling specs (`date-picker.md`, `time-field.md`) were already
migrated to the real conventions. Per CLAUDE.md ("resolve spec/code mismatch in the same task"),
this PR must rewrite those sections to match reality. Concrete drift to fix:

- Uses a nonexistent `DateTime` type. Real type is `ars_i18n::CalendarDateTime` with **private**
  `date`/`time` fields, ctor `CalendarDateTime::new(date, time)`, accessors `.date()`/`.time()`.
- `maybe_publish` compares values with `<`/`>`, but `CalendarDate` does **not** derive `Ord`.
  Must compare via `CalendarDate::compare(&self, other) -> Ordering` then `Time::cmp` (Time is Ord).
- Effects use idealized `PendingEffect::new(name, closure)` + `use_platform_effects()` + `no_cleanup()`.
  Real convention (date_picker/time_field): a typed `Effect` enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`,
  and `type Effect = Effect;` on the Machine impl. Adapters dispatch on `effect.name`.
- `Context` `root_attrs` reads `ctx.parsed_date` / `ctx.parsed_time` / `ctx.format` — none exist.
- `assemble_date` uses `NonZero` month/day; real `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year: i32, month: u8,
  day: u8) -> Result<_, _>` (plain `u8`, returns `Result`).
- `assemble_time` calls `Time::new(h,m,s)`; real is `Time::new(h,m,s, millis: u16) -> Result<_, _>`.
- `kind.as_str()` / `kind.aria_label()` (no-arg) — real: `DateSegmentKind::data_name(self)` (`pub(crate)`)
  and `aria_label(&self, &Messages, &Locale)`.
- `HourCycle::has_day_period()` / `display_hour_range()` — not methods; they are free fns in time_field.
- `KeyboardKey::Char(ch)` does not exist; segment typing flows via `KeyboardEventData.character`
  (TimeField pattern: `Api::on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`).

## Key types & reuse (verified)

- Segments: `date_time::date_field::segment::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}` (re-exported at
  `date_field::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}`). Ctors `DateSegment::new_numeric(kind, min, max, &str)`,
  `new_literal(&str)`, `new_non_editable(kind, &str)`. `DateSegmentKind::{is_editable, is_numeric, data_name}`.
- Date order: `ars_i18n::{date_order, DateOrder}` (CLDR-backed) — reuse for locale segment order +
  the `.`/`/` separator heuristic from `date_picker::default_format_for_locale`.
- `CalendarDate`: `new_gregorian`, `.year()/.month()/.day()`, `.compare(&self,&Self)->Ordering`.
- `Time`: `new(h,m,s,ms)->Result`, `.hour()/.minute()/.second()`, derives `Ord`.
- `CalendarDateTime::{new, date, time}`. `HourCycle::{H11,H12,H23,H24}`. `TimeGranularity` (Ord).
- `Bindable::{controlled, uncontrolled, get, set, sync_controlled}`; `ComponentIds::{from_id, id,
  part, item}` (`item(part, &key)`); `Env::new(locale, Arc<dyn IntlBackend>)`, `env.locale`,
  `env.intl_backend`; `locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend)`.
- `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &messages)`, `svc.connect(&send)`, `svc.take_initial_effects()`.
- `#[derive(ars_core::ComponentPart)] #[scope="..."]`; `Part::X.data_attrs()` → 2 `(HtmlAttr,_)` pairs.
- Snapshot helper (per-file, copy it): `fn snapshot_attrs(a:&AttrMap)->String { format!("{a:#?}") }`,
  asserted with `insta::assert_snapshot!("name", snapshot_attrs(&api.x_attrs()))`. Test env uses
  `StubIntlBackend` + `en_us()` locale (mirror `date_picker/tests.rs`).

## Approach

### 1. Shared hour-cycle helpers (per user decision: promote to shared module)
- New `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/hour_cycle.rs` (`pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;` in
  `date_time/mod.rs`). Move from `time_field/mod.rs`: `display_hour_range`, `has_day_period`,
  `display_hour`, `display_hour_to_24`, `is_pm`, and `digits_needed`. Make them `pub(crate)`.
- Refactor `time_field/mod.rs` to `use super::hour_cycle::*` and delete the local copies. Run
  TimeField's existing tests to confirm no behavior change.

### 2. New machine — `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/mod.rs`
Mirror DatePicker/TimeField structure. Public items (all documented for `missing_docs`):
- `State` { Idle, Focused, Open }, `Event` (per spec §1.2; replace `KeyDown{key}` handling so
  segment typing comes through the Api via `KeyboardEventData`), `Context`, `Props` (add
  `today: CalendarDate` injected by adapter — forwarded to `calendar_props`, default like Calendar's
  `2025-01-01`), `Messages` (spec's 5 fields **plus** per-segment label fields:
  `year_label/month_label/day_label/hour_label/minute_label/second_label/day_period_label` —
  user decision), and a typed `Effect` enum
  { OpenChange, ValueChange, FocusCalendar, RestoreFocusToTrigger, FocusFirstTimeSegment, TypeBufferCommit }.
- Context fields: `value: Bindable<Option<CalendarDateTime>>`, `date_value: Option<CalendarDate>`,
  `time_value: Option<Time>`, `focused_segment`, `open`, `date_segments`, `time_segments`,
  `type_buffer`, `locale`, `messages`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`, `granularity`,
  `hour_cycle`, `min_value/max_value: Option<CalendarDateTime>`, `disabled/readonly/required/invalid`,
  `is_touched`, `name`, `is_rtl`, `today`, `ids`. Keep the spec's Context helper methods
  (`all_segments`, `first_editable`, `next_editable_after`, `prev_editable_before`, `is_date_segment`,
  `is_time_segment`, `set_segment_value`, `clear_segment_value`, `increment/decrement_segment`,
  `is_complete`, `assemble_date`, `assemble_time`, `assemble_datetime`) but corrected to real APIs
  (Gregorian assembly via `new_gregorian(...).ok()`; time via `hour_cycle::display_hour_to_24` +
  `Time::new(...,0).ok()`).
- `Machine`: `init` (build date segments via `date_order`, time segments via `hour_cycle` helpers,
  resolve cycle via `props.hour_cycle.unwrap_or(locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend))`), `transition`
  (port spec arms; emit `PendingEffect::named`), `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps` for controlled
  `value`/`min`/`max`/`disabled` sync (mirror DatePicker), `connect`.
- `cmp_datetime(a,b)` helper: `a.date().compare(b.date())` then `a.time().cmp(b.time())`;
  `maybe_publish` clamps with it.
- `Part` enum + `Api` with all `*_attrs()` getters and event handlers per spec §1.8, corrected:
  `segment_attrs` id via `ids.item("segment", &kind.data_name())`, label via local resolver over the
  new `Messages` segment fields (TimeField's `time_segment_aria_label` pattern); `root_attrs` announce
  label built from a local `format_datetime(date,time,locale)` (reuse date_order pattern + h:m[:s]),
  not the bogus `parsed_*`/`format`; `hidden_input_attrs` ISO via
  `format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", date.year(), date.month(), date.day(), time.hour(),
  time.minute(), time.second())`; `calendar_props()` builds `calendar::Props { id:
  format!("{}-calendar", ids.id()), value: Some(date_value.clone()), min/max from min_value/max_value
  `.map(|dt| dt.date().clone())`, disabled, readonly, is_rtl, visible_months, today, ..Default }`.
  `on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`; `on_trigger_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`;
  `on_content_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`. `ConnectApi for Api` maps every `Part`.

### 3. Wire module
- `date_time/mod.rs`: add `pub mod date_time_picker;` and `pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;`.

### 4. Tests (TDD — write first) — `date_time_picker/tests.rs`
Named tests from the issue, plus conformance & snapshots:
- Date segments **and** time segments both present; trigger + calendar `Content` parts exist.
- Combined value renders ISO `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` in `hidden_input_attrs`.
- `CalendarSelectDate` preserves existing time segments/value; date-only updates date.
- Editing a time segment (type-ahead / increment) preserves existing date value.
- Form integration: `name` + ISO value on hidden input.
- Min/max `CalendarDateTime` clamping via `maybe_publish` across date+time.
- ConnectApi: `Control` `role=group`+`aria-labelledby`; `DateSegmentGroup`/`TimeSegmentGroup`
  `role=group` + `aria-label`; `aria-required`/`aria-invalid` on date group when set.
- Spec-conformance: `Part` enum variants/anatomy + spinbutton attrs (`role`, `aria-valuemin/max/now/text`).
- Snapshot tests for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state/prop/context branch
  (idle/focused/open, disabled, readonly, invalid, rtl, with/without value, clear-trigger
  hidden-when-empty, h12 vs h23, granularity second), committed `.snap` under `snapshots/`,
  passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

### 5. Spec sync (same PR)
Rewrite `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`:
- §1.3 Context: `CalendarDateTime`, add `intl_backend`, `today`, `invalid`, corrected helper bodies.
- §1.4 Props: add `today`; keep `calendar`/`hour_cycle`/`visible_months`.
- §1.7: typed `Effect` enum, `PendingEffect::named`, `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps`, `date_order`
  segment build, `hour_cycle` helpers, `cmp_datetime` clamping, `Time::new(...,0)`/`new_gregorian`.
- §1.8: corrected `root_attrs` announce, `segment_attrs` (data_name + Messages labels),
  `hidden_input_attrs` ISO, `calendar_props`, `KeyboardEventData`-based handlers.
- §4.1 Messages: document the added per-segment label fields.
- Add the `> Implementation note:` callout (mirroring date-picker §1 header) explaining the typed
  Effect/`PendingEffect::named` convention vs adapter focus handling.

### 6. Mandatory audit
Run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (3 sequential audits — spec/impl drift, iterative
"anything missing", test-coverage) and land all findings in this PR.

## Critical files
- New: `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/{mod.rs,tests.rs,snapshots/*.snap}`
- New: `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/hour_cycle.rs`
- Modify: `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/time_field/mod.rs` (use promoted helpers)
- Modify: `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs` (declare new modules)
- Modify: `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md` (sync drift)
- Reference (do not modify): `date_picker/mod.rs`, `date_field/mod.rs`, `time_field/mod.rs`,
  `calendar/mod.rs`, `date_field/segment.rs`.

## Verification
1. `cargo test -p ars-components date_time::date_time_picker` — unit + conformance pass, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — snapshots reviewed & complete.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components date_time::time_field` — refactor caused no TimeField regression.
4. `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec frontmatter still consistent.
5. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast` (fmt, clippy, unit, native coverage) green before push.
6. PR body `Closes #292`; add `snapshot-reviewed` label (new `.snap` fixtures); then run the
   `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop until Codex 👍.

  `year_label/month_label/day_label/hour_label/minute_label/second_label/day_period_label` —
  user decision), and a typed `Effect` enum
  { OpenChange, ValueChange, FocusCalendar, RestoreFocusToTrigger, FocusFirstTimeSegment, TypeBufferCommit }.
- Context fields: `value: Bindable<Option<CalendarDateTime>>`, `date_value: Option<CalendarDate>`,
  `time_value: Option<Time>`, `focused_segment`, `open`, `date_segments`, `time_segments`,
  `type_buffer`, `locale`, `messages`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`, `granularity`,
  `hour_cycle`, `min_value/max_value: Option<CalendarDateTime>`, `disabled/readonly/required/invalid`,
  `is_touched`, `name`, `is_rtl`, `today`, `ids`. Keep the spec's Context helper methods
  (`all_segments`, `first_editable`, `next_editable_after`, `prev_editable_before`, `is_date_segment`,
  `is_time_segment`, `set_segment_value`, `clear_segment_value`, `increment/decrement_segment`,
  `is_complete`, `assemble_date`, `assemble_time`, `assemble_datetime`) but corrected to real APIs
  (Gregorian assembly via `new_gregorian(...).ok()`; time via `hour_cycle::display_hour_to_24` +
  `Time::new(...,0).ok()`).
- `Machine`: `init` (build date segments via `date_order`, time segments via `hour_cycle` helpers,
  resolve cycle via `props.hour_cycle.unwrap_or(locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend))`), `transition`
  (port spec arms; emit `PendingEffect::named`), `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps` for controlled
  `value`/`min`/`max`/`disabled` sync (mirror DatePicker), `connect`.
- `cmp_datetime(a,b)` helper: `a.date().compare(b.date())` then `a.time().cmp(b.time())`;
  `maybe_publish` clamps with it.
- `Part` enum + `Api` with all `*_attrs()` getters and event handlers per spec §1.8, corrected:
  `segment_attrs` id via `ids.item("segment", &kind.data_name())`, label via local resolver over the
  new `Messages` segment fields (TimeField's `time_segment_aria_label` pattern); `root_attrs` announce
  label built from a local `format_datetime(date,time,locale)` (reuse date_order pattern + h:m[:s]),
  not the bogus `parsed_*`/`format`; `hidden_input_attrs` ISO via
  `format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", date.year(), date.month(), date.day(), time.hour(),
  time.minute(), time.second())`; `calendar_props()` builds `calendar::Props { id:
  format!("{}-calendar", ids.id()), value: Some(date_value.clone()), min/max from min_value/max_value
  `.map(|dt| dt.date().clone())`, disabled, readonly, is_rtl, visible_months, today, ..Default }`.
  `on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`; `on_trigger_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`;
  `on_content_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`. `ConnectApi for Api` maps every `Part`.

### 3. Wire module
- `date_time/mod.rs`: add `pub mod date_time_picker;` and `pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;`.

### 4. Tests (TDD — write first) — `date_time_picker/tests.rs`
Named tests from the issue, plus conformance & snapshots:
- Date segments **and** time segments both present; trigger + calendar `Content` parts exist.
- Combined value renders ISO `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` in `hidden_input_attrs`.
- `CalendarSelectDate` preserves existing time segments/value; date-only updates date.
- Editing a time segment (type-ahead / increment) preserves existing date value.
- Form integration: `name` + ISO value on hidden input.
- Min/max `CalendarDateTime` clamping via `maybe_publish` across date+time.
- ConnectApi: `Control` `role=group`+`aria-labelledby`; `DateSegmentGroup`/`TimeSegmentGroup`
  `role=group` + `aria-label`; `aria-required`/`aria-invalid` on date group when set.
- Spec-conformance: `Part` enum variants/anatomy + spinbutton attrs (`role`, `aria-valuemin/max/now/text`).
- Snapshot tests for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state/prop/context branch
  (idle/focused/open, disabled, readonly, invalid, rtl, with/without value, clear-trigger
  hidden-when-empty, h12 vs h23, granularity second), committed `.snap` under `snapshots/`,
  passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

### 5. Spec sync (same PR)
Rewrite `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`:

- §4.1 Messages: document the added per-segment label fields.

# Task #292 — Implement DateTimePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #292 (Epic #224) asks for the framework-agnostic core of `DateTimePicker`: a
composite that unifies a **segmented date input** (like `DateField`), a **segmented time input**
(like `TimeField`), and a **calendar popover + trigger** (like `DatePicker`) into a single state
machine that owns both date and time segments and produces a combined `CalendarDateTime` value.
Dependencies #272 (TimeField) and #289 (DatePicker) are both merged. Scope is the agnostic
machine only — no Leptos/Dioxus adapters. Test tier: Unit (+ snapshots + spec-conformance).

**Important finding — the spec is heavily drifted.** `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`
§1.3/§1.7/§1.8/§4.1 was written against idealized pseudo-code that does **not** compile against
the real workspace API, while the sibling specs (`date-picker.md`, `time-field.md`) were already
migrated to the real conventions. Per CLAUDE.md ("resolve spec/code mismatch in the same task"),
this PR must rewrite those sections to match reality. Concrete drift to fix:

- Uses a nonexistent `DateTime` type. Real type is `ars_i18n::CalendarDateTime` with **private**
  `date`/`time` fields, ctor `CalendarDateTime::new(date, time)`, accessors `.date()`/`.time()`.
- `maybe_publish` compares values with `<`/`>`, but `CalendarDate` does **not** derive `Ord`.
  Must compare via `CalendarDate::compare(&self, other) -> Ordering` then `Time::cmp` (Time is Ord).
- Effects use idealized `PendingEffect::new(name, closure)` + `use_platform_effects()` + `no_cleanup()`.
  Real convention (date_picker/time_field): a typed `Effect` enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`,
  and `type Effect = Effect;` on the Machine impl. Adapters dispatch on `effect.name`.
- `Context` `root_attrs` reads `ctx.parsed_date` / `ctx.parsed_time` / `ctx.format` — none exist.
- `assemble_date` uses `NonZero` month/day; real `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year: i32, month: u8,
  day: u8) -> Result<_, _>` (plain `u8`, returns `Result`).
- `assemble_time` calls `Time::new(h,m,s)`; real is `Time::new(h,m,s, millis: u16) -> Result<_, _>`.
- `kind.as_str()` / `kind.aria_label()` (no-arg) — real: `DateSegmentKind::data_name(self)` (`pub(crate)`)
  and `aria_label(&self, &Messages, &Locale)`.
- `HourCycle::has_day_period()` / `display_hour_range()` — not methods; they are free fns in time_field.
- `KeyboardKey::Char(ch)` does not exist; segment typing flows via `KeyboardEventData.character`
  (TimeField pattern: `Api::on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`).

## Key types & reuse (verified)

- Segments: `date_time::date_field::segment::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}` (re-exported at
  `date_field::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}`). Ctors `DateSegment::new_numeric(kind, min, max, &str)`,
  `new_literal(&str)`, `new_non_editable(kind, &str)`. `DateSegmentKind::{is_editable, is_numeric, data_name}`.
- Date order: `ars_i18n::{date_order, DateOrder}` (CLDR-backed) — reuse for locale segment order +
  the `.`/`/` separator heuristic from `date_picker::default_format_for_locale`.
- `CalendarDate`: `new_gregorian`, `.year()/.month()/.day()`, `.compare(&self,&Self)->Ordering`.
- `Time`: `new(h,m,s,ms)->Result`, `.hour()/.minute()/.second()`, derives `Ord`.
- `CalendarDateTime::{new, date, time}`. `HourCycle::{H11,H12,H23,H24}`. `TimeGranularity` (Ord).
- `Bindable::{controlled, uncontrolled, get, set, sync_controlled}`; `ComponentIds::{from_id, id,
  part, item}` (`item(part, &key)`); `Env::new(locale, Arc<dyn IntlBackend>)`, `env.locale`,
  `env.intl_backend`; `locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend)`.
- `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &messages)`, `svc.connect(&send)`, `svc.take_initial_effects()`.
- `#[derive(ars_core::ComponentPart)] #[scope="..."]`; `Part::X.data_attrs()` → 2 `(HtmlAttr,_)` pairs.
- Snapshot helper (per-file, copy it): `fn snapshot_attrs(a:&AttrMap)->String { format!("{a:#?}") }`,
  asserted with `insta::assert_snapshot!("name", snapshot_attrs(&api.x_attrs()))`. Test env uses
  `StubIntlBackend` + `en_us()` locale (mirror `date_picker/tests.rs`).

## Approach

### 1. Shared hour-cycle helpers (per user decision: promote to shared module)
- New `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/hour_cycle.rs` (`pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;` in
  `date_time/mod.rs`). Move from `time_field/mod.rs`: `display_hour_range`, `has_day_period`,
  `display_hour`, `display_hour_to_24`, `is_pm`, and `digits_needed`. Make them `pub(crate)`.
- Refactor `time_field/mod.rs` to `use super::hour_cycle::*` and delete the local copies. Run
  TimeField's existing tests to confirm no behavior change.

### 2. New machine — `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/mod.rs`
Mirror DatePicker/TimeField structure. Public items (all documented for `missing_docs`):
- `State` { Idle, Focused, Open }, `Event` (per spec §1.2; replace `KeyDown{key}` handling so
  segment typing comes through the Api via `KeyboardEventData`), `Context`, `Props` (add
  `today: CalendarDate` injected by adapter — forwarded to `calendar_props`, default like Calendar's
  `2025-01-01`), `Messages` (spec's 5 fields **plus** per-segment label fields:
  `year_label/month_label/day_label/hour_label/minute_label/second_label/day_period_label` —
  user decision), and a typed `Effect` enum
  { OpenChange, ValueChange, FocusCalendar, RestoreFocusToTrigger, FocusFirstTimeSegment, TypeBufferCommit }.
- Context fields: `value: Bindable<Option<CalendarDateTime>>`, `date_value: Option<CalendarDate>`,
  `time_value: Option<Time>`, `focused_segment`, `open`, `date_segments`, `time_segments`,
  `type_buffer`, `locale`, `messages`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`, `granularity`,
  `hour_cycle`, `min_value/max_value: Option<CalendarDateTime>`, `disabled/readonly/required/invalid`,
  `is_touched`, `name`, `is_rtl`, `today`, `ids`. Keep the spec's Context helper methods
  (`all_segments`, `first_editable`, `next_editable_after`, `prev_editable_before`, `is_date_segment`,
  `is_time_segment`, `set_segment_value`, `clear_segment_value`, `increment/decrement_segment`,
  `is_complete`, `assemble_date`, `assemble_time`, `assemble_datetime`) but corrected to real APIs
  (Gregorian assembly via `new_gregorian(...).ok()`; time via `hour_cycle::display_hour_to_24` +
  `Time::new(...,0).ok()`).
- `Machine`: `init` (build date segments via `date_order`, time segments via `hour_cycle` helpers,
  resolve cycle via `props.hour_cycle.unwrap_or(locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend))`), `transition`
  (port spec arms; emit `PendingEffect::named`), `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps` for controlled
  `value`/`min`/`max`/`disabled` sync (mirror DatePicker), `connect`.
- `cmp_datetime(a,b)` helper: `a.date().compare(b.date())` then `a.time().cmp(b.time())`;
  `maybe_publish` clamps with it.
- `Part` enum + `Api` with all `*_attrs()` getters and event handlers per spec §1.8, corrected:
  `segment_attrs` id via `ids.item("segment", &kind.data_name())`, label via local resolver over the
  new `Messages` segment fields (TimeField's `time_segment_aria_label` pattern); `root_attrs` announce
  label built from a local `format_datetime(date,time,locale)` (reuse date_order pattern + h:m[:s]),
  not the bogus `parsed_*`/`format`; `hidden_input_attrs` ISO via
  `format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", date.year(), date.month(), date.day(), time.hour(),
  time.minute(), time.second())`; `calendar_props()` builds `calendar::Props { id:
  format!("{}-calendar", ids.id()), value: Some(date_value.clone()), min/max from min_value/max_value
  `.map(|dt| dt.date().clone())`, disabled, readonly, is_rtl, visible_months, today, ..Default }`.
  `on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`; `on_trigger_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`;
  `on_content_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`. `ConnectApi for Api` maps every `Part`.

### 3. Wire module
- `date_time/mod.rs`: add `pub mod date_time_picker;` and `pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;`.

### 4. Tests (TDD — write first) — `date_time_picker/tests.rs`
Named tests from the issue, plus conformance & snapshots:
- Date segments **and** time segments both present; trigger + calendar `Content` parts exist.

  `hour_cycle`, `min_value/max_value: Option<CalendarDateTime>`, `disabled/readonly/required/invalid`,
  `is_touched`, `name`, `is_rtl`, `today`, `ids`. Keep the spec's Context helper methods
  (`all_segments`, `first_editable`, `next_editable_after`, `prev_editable_before`, `is_date_segment`,
  `is_time_segment`, `set_segment_value`, `clear_segment_value`, `increment/decrement_segment`,
  `is_complete`, `assemble_date`, `assemble_time`, `assemble_datetime`) but corrected to real APIs
  (Gregorian assembly via `new_gregorian(...).ok()`; time via `hour_cycle::display_hour_to_24` +
  `Time::new(...,0).ok()`).
- `Machine`: `init` (build date segments via `date_order`, time segments via `hour_cycle` helpers,
  resolve cycle via `props.hour_cycle.unwrap_or(locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend))`), `transition`
  (port spec arms; emit `PendingEffect::named`), `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps` for controlled
  `value`/`min`/`max`/`disabled` sync (mirror DatePicker), `connect`.
- `cmp_datetime(a,b)` helper: `a.date().compare(b.date())` then `a.time().cmp(b.time())`;
  `maybe_publish` clamps with it.
- `Part` enum + `Api` with all `*_attrs()` getters and event handlers per spec §1.8, corrected:
  `segment_attrs` id via `ids.item("segment", &kind.data_name())`, label via local resolver over the
  new `Messages` segment fields (TimeField's `time_segment_aria_label` pattern); `root_attrs` announce
  label built from a local `format_datetime(date,time,locale)` (reuse date_order pattern + h:m[:s]),
  not the bogus `parsed_*`/`format`; `hidden_input_attrs` ISO via
  `format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", date.year(), date.month(), date.day(), time.hour(),
  time.minute(), time.second())`; `calendar_props()` builds `calendar::Props { id:
  format!("{}-calendar", ids.id()), value: Some(date_value.clone()), min/max from min_value/max_value
  `.map(|dt| dt.date().clone())`, disabled, readonly, is_rtl, visible_months, today, ..Default }`.
  `on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`; `on_trigger_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`;
  `on_content_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`. `ConnectApi for Api` maps every `Part`.

### 3. Wire module
- `date_time/mod.rs`: add `pub mod date_time_picker;` and `pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;`.

### 4. Tests (TDD — write first) — `date_time_picker/tests.rs`
Named tests from the issue, plus conformance & snapshots:
- Date segments **and** time segments both present; trigger + calendar `Content` parts exist.
- Combined value renders ISO `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` in `hidden_input_attrs`.
- `CalendarSelectDate` preserves existing time segments/value; date-only updates date.
- Editing a time segment (type-ahead / increment) preserves existing date value.
- Form integration: `name` + ISO value on hidden input.
- Min/max `CalendarDateTime` clamping via `maybe_publish` across date+time.
- ConnectApi: `Control` `role=group`+`aria-labelledby`; `DateSegmentGroup`/`TimeSegmentGroup`
  `role=group` + `aria-label`; `aria-required`/`aria-invalid` on date group when set.
- Spec-conformance: `Part` enum variants/anatomy + spinbutton attrs (`role`, `aria-valuemin/max/now/text`).
- Snapshot tests for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state/prop/context branch
  (idle/focused/open, disabled, readonly, invalid, rtl, with/without value, clear-trigger
  hidden-when-empty, h12 vs h23, granularity second), committed `.snap` under `snapshots/`,
  passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

### 5. Spec sync (same PR)
Rewrite `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`:
- §1.3 Context: `CalendarDateTime`, add `intl_backend`, `today`, `invalid`, corrected helper bodies.
- §1.4 Props: add `today`; keep `calendar`/`hour_cycle`/`visible_months`.
- §1.7: typed `Effect` enum, `PendingEffect::named`, `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps`, `date_order`
  segment build, `hour_cycle` helpers, `cmp_datetime` clamping, `Time::new(...,0)`/`new_gregorian`.
- §1.8: corrected `root_attrs` announce, `segment_attrs` (data_name + Messages labels),
  `hidden_input_attrs` ISO, `calendar_props`, `KeyboardEventData`-based handlers.
- §4.1 Messages: document the added per-segment label fields.
- Add the `> Implementation note:` callout (mirroring date-picker §1 header) explaining the typed
  Effect/`PendingEffect::named` convention vs adapter focus handling.

### 6. Mandatory audit
Run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (3 sequential audits — spec/impl drift, iterative
"anything missing", test-coverage) and land all findings in this PR.

# Task #292 — Implement DateTimePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #292 (Epic #224) asks for the framework-agnostic core of `DateTimePicker`: a
composite that unifies a **segmented date input** (like `DateField`), a **segmented time input**
(like `TimeField`), and a **calendar popover + trigger** (like `DatePicker`) into a single state
machine that owns both date and time segments and produces a combined `CalendarDateTime` value.
Dependencies #272 (TimeField) and #289 (DatePicker) are both merged. Scope is the agnostic
machine only — no Leptos/Dioxus adapters. Test tier: Unit (+ snapshots + spec-conformance).

**Important finding — the spec is heavily drifted.** `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`
§1.3/§1.7/§1.8/§4.1 was written against idealized pseudo-code that does **not** compile against
the real workspace API, while the sibling specs (`date-picker.md`, `time-field.md`) were already
migrated to the real conventions. Per CLAUDE.md ("resolve spec/code mismatch in the same task"),
this PR must rewrite those sections to match reality. Concrete drift to fix:

- Uses a nonexistent `DateTime` type. Real type is `ars_i18n::CalendarDateTime` with **private**
  `date`/`time` fields, ctor `CalendarDateTime::new(date, time)`, accessors `.date()`/`.time()`.
- `maybe_publish` compares values with `<`/`>`, but `CalendarDate` does **not** derive `Ord`.
  Must compare via `CalendarDate::compare(&self, other) -> Ordering` then `Time::cmp` (Time is Ord).
- Effects use idealized `PendingEffect::new(name, closure)` + `use_platform_effects()` + `no_cleanup()`.
  Real convention (date_picker/time_field): a typed `Effect` enum + `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`,
  and `type Effect = Effect;` on the Machine impl. Adapters dispatch on `effect.name`.
- `Context` `root_attrs` reads `ctx.parsed_date` / `ctx.parsed_time` / `ctx.format` — none exist.
- `assemble_date` uses `NonZero` month/day; real `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year: i32, month: u8,
  day: u8) -> Result<_, _>` (plain `u8`, returns `Result`).
- `assemble_time` calls `Time::new(h,m,s)`; real is `Time::new(h,m,s, millis: u16) -> Result<_, _>`.
- `kind.as_str()` / `kind.aria_label()` (no-arg) — real: `DateSegmentKind::data_name(self)` (`pub(crate)`)
  and `aria_label(&self, &Messages, &Locale)`.
- `HourCycle::has_day_period()` / `display_hour_range()` — not methods; they are free fns in time_field.
- `KeyboardKey::Char(ch)` does not exist; segment typing flows via `KeyboardEventData.character`
  (TimeField pattern: `Api::on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`).

## Key types & reuse (verified)

- Segments: `date_time::date_field::segment::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}` (re-exported at
  `date_field::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind}`). Ctors `DateSegment::new_numeric(kind, min, max, &str)`,
  `new_literal(&str)`, `new_non_editable(kind, &str)`. `DateSegmentKind::{is_editable, is_numeric, data_name}`.
- Date order: `ars_i18n::{date_order, DateOrder}` (CLDR-backed) — reuse for locale segment order +
  the `.`/`/` separator heuristic from `date_picker::default_format_for_locale`.
- `CalendarDate`: `new_gregorian`, `.year()/.month()/.day()`, `.compare(&self,&Self)->Ordering`.
- `Time`: `new(h,m,s,ms)->Result`, `.hour()/.minute()/.second()`, derives `Ord`.
- `CalendarDateTime::{new, date, time}`. `HourCycle::{H11,H12,H23,H24}`. `TimeGranularity` (Ord).
- `Bindable::{controlled, uncontrolled, get, set, sync_controlled}`; `ComponentIds::{from_id, id,
  part, item}` (`item(part, &key)`); `Env::new(locale, Arc<dyn IntlBackend>)`, `env.locale`,
  `env.intl_backend`; `locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend)`.
- `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &messages)`, `svc.connect(&send)`, `svc.take_initial_effects()`.
- `#[derive(ars_core::ComponentPart)] #[scope="..."]`; `Part::X.data_attrs()` → 2 `(HtmlAttr,_)` pairs.
- Snapshot helper (per-file, copy it): `fn snapshot_attrs(a:&AttrMap)->String { format!("{a:#?}") }`,
  asserted with `insta::assert_snapshot!("name", snapshot_attrs(&api.x_attrs()))`. Test env uses
  `StubIntlBackend` + `en_us()` locale (mirror `date_picker/tests.rs`).

## Approach

### 1. Shared hour-cycle helpers (per user decision: promote to shared module)
- New `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/hour_cycle.rs` (`pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;` in
  `date_time/mod.rs`). Move from `time_field/mod.rs`: `display_hour_range`, `has_day_period`,
  `display_hour`, `display_hour_to_24`, `is_pm`, and `digits_needed`. Make them `pub(crate)`.
- Refactor `time_field/mod.rs` to `use super::hour_cycle::*` and delete the local copies. Run
  TimeField's existing tests to confirm no behavior change.

### 2. New machine — `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/mod.rs`
Mirror DatePicker/TimeField structure. Public items (all documented for `missing_docs`):
- `State` { Idle, Focused, Open }, `Event` (per spec §1.2; replace `KeyDown{key}` handling so
  segment typing comes through the Api via `KeyboardEventData`), `Context`, `Props` (add
  `today: CalendarDate` injected by adapter — forwarded to `calendar_props`, default like Calendar's
  `2025-01-01`), `Messages` (spec's 5 fields **plus** per-segment label fields:
  `year_label/month_label/day_label/hour_label/minute_label/second_label/day_period_label` —
  user decision), and a typed `Effect` enum
  { OpenChange, ValueChange, FocusCalendar, RestoreFocusToTrigger, FocusFirstTimeSegment, TypeBufferCommit }.
- Context fields: `value: Bindable<Option<CalendarDateTime>>`, `date_value: Option<CalendarDate>`,
  `time_value: Option<Time>`, `focused_segment`, `open`, `date_segments`, `time_segments`,
  `type_buffer`, `locale`, `messages`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`, `granularity`,
  `hour_cycle`, `min_value/max_value: Option<CalendarDateTime>`, `disabled/readonly/required/invalid`,
  `is_touched`, `name`, `is_rtl`, `today`, `ids`. Keep the spec's Context helper methods
  (`all_segments`, `first_editable`, `next_editable_after`, `prev_editable_before`, `is_date_segment`,
  `is_time_segment`, `set_segment_value`, `clear_segment_value`, `increment/decrement_segment`,
  `is_complete`, `assemble_date`, `assemble_time`, `assemble_datetime`) but corrected to real APIs
  (Gregorian assembly via `new_gregorian(...).ok()`; time via `hour_cycle::display_hour_to_24` +
  `Time::new(...,0).ok()`).
- `Machine`: `init` (build date segments via `date_order`, time segments via `hour_cycle` helpers,
  resolve cycle via `props.hour_cycle.unwrap_or(locale.hour_cycle(&*env.intl_backend))`), `transition`
  (port spec arms; emit `PendingEffect::named`), `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps` for controlled
  `value`/`min`/`max`/`disabled` sync (mirror DatePicker), `connect`.
- `cmp_datetime(a,b)` helper: `a.date().compare(b.date())` then `a.time().cmp(b.time())`;
  `maybe_publish` clamps with it.
- `Part` enum + `Api` with all `*_attrs()` getters and event handlers per spec §1.8, corrected:
  `segment_attrs` id via `ids.item("segment", &kind.data_name())`, label via local resolver over the
  new `Messages` segment fields (TimeField's `time_segment_aria_label` pattern); `root_attrs` announce
  label built from a local `format_datetime(date,time,locale)` (reuse date_order pattern + h:m[:s]),
  not the bogus `parsed_*`/`format`; `hidden_input_attrs` ISO via
  `format!("{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}", date.year(), date.month(), date.day(), time.hour(),
  time.minute(), time.second())`; `calendar_props()` builds `calendar::Props { id:
  format!("{}-calendar", ids.id()), value: Some(date_value.clone()), min/max from min_value/max_value
  `.map(|dt| dt.date().clone())`, disabled, readonly, is_rtl, visible_months, today, ..Default }`.
  `on_segment_keydown(kind, &KeyboardEventData)`; `on_trigger_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`;
  `on_content_keydown(&KeyboardEventData)`. `ConnectApi for Api` maps every `Part`.

### 3. Wire module
- `date_time/mod.rs`: add `pub mod date_time_picker;` and `pub(crate) mod hour_cycle;`.

### 4. Tests (TDD — write first) — `date_time_picker/tests.rs`
Named tests from the issue, plus conformance & snapshots:
- Date segments **and** time segments both present; trigger + calendar `Content` parts exist.
- Combined value renders ISO `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` in `hidden_input_attrs`.
- `CalendarSelectDate` preserves existing time segments/value; date-only updates date.
- Editing a time segment (type-ahead / increment) preserves existing date value.
- Form integration: `name` + ISO value on hidden input.
- Min/max `CalendarDateTime` clamping via `maybe_publish` across date+time.
- ConnectApi: `Control` `role=group`+`aria-labelledby`; `DateSegmentGroup`/`TimeSegmentGroup`
  `role=group` + `aria-label`; `aria-required`/`aria-invalid` on date group when set.
- Spec-conformance: `Part` enum variants/anatomy + spinbutton attrs (`role`, `aria-valuemin/max/now/text`).
- Snapshot tests for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state/prop/context branch
  (idle/focused/open, disabled, readonly, invalid, rtl, with/without value, clear-trigger
  hidden-when-empty, h12 vs h23, granularity second), committed `.snap` under `snapshots/`,
  passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

### 5. Spec sync (same PR)
Rewrite `spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md`:
- §1.3 Context: `CalendarDateTime`, add `intl_backend`, `today`, `invalid`, corrected helper bodies.
- §1.4 Props: add `today`; keep `calendar`/`hour_cycle`/`visible_months`.
- §1.7: typed `Effect` enum, `PendingEffect::named`, `on_props_changed`/`SyncProps`, `date_order`
  segment build, `hour_cycle` helpers, `cmp_datetime` clamping, `Time::new(...,0)`/`new_gregorian`.
- §1.8: corrected `root_attrs` announce, `segment_attrs` (data_name + Messages labels),
  `hidden_input_attrs` ISO, `calendar_props`, `KeyboardEventData`-based handlers.
- §4.1 Messages: document the added per-segment label fields.
- Add the `> Implementation note:` callout (mirroring date-picker §1 header) explaining the typed
  Effect/`PendingEffect::named` convention vs adapter focus handling.

### 6. Mandatory audit
Run the `post-implementation-audit` skill (3 sequential audits — spec/impl drift, iterative
"anything missing", test-coverage) and land all findings in this PR.

## Critical files
- New: `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/{mod.rs,tests.rs,snapshots/*.snap}`

1	//! TimeField component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! This module implements the framework-agnostic segmented time input defined
4	//! in `spec/components/date-time/time-field.md`.
5	
6	#[cfg(test)]
7	mod tests;
8	
9	use alloc::{
10	    boxed::Box,
11	    string::{String, ToString},
12	    sync::Arc,
13	    vec,
14	    vec::Vec,
15	};
16	use core::{
17	    fmt::{self, Debug},
18	    num::NonZeroU8,
19	};
20	
21	use ars_core::{
22	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi,
23	    Direction, Env, HtmlAttr, IntlBackend, KeyboardKey, Locale, MessageFn, PendingEffect,
24	    TransitionPlan,
25	};
26	use ars_i18n::{HourCycle, Time};
27	use ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData;
28	
29	use super::date_field::segment::{DateSegment, DateSegmentKind};
30	
31	/// States for the `TimeField` component.
32	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
33	pub enum State {
34	    /// The field has no focused segment.
35	    Idle,
36	
37	    /// A specific editable time segment has focus.
38	    Focused(DateSegmentKind),
39	}
40	
41	/// Events for the `TimeField` component.
42	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
43	pub enum Event {
44	    /// Focus moved to a segment.
45	    FocusSegment {
46	        /// The segment that received focus.
47	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
48	    },
49	
50	    /// Focus left the entire field group.
51	    BlurAll,
52	
53	    /// Increment a focused segment by one unit, wrapping within its range.
54	    IncrementSegment {
55	        /// The segment to increment.
56	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
57	    },
58	
59	    /// Decrement a focused segment by one unit, wrapping within its range.
60	    DecrementSegment {
61	        /// The segment to decrement.
62	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
63	    },
64	
65	    /// Type a printable character into the focused segment.
66	    TypeIntoSegment {
67	        /// The segment receiving typed input.
68	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
69	
70	        /// The typed character.
71	        ch: char,
72	    },
73	
74	    /// Commit the current type-ahead buffer for a segment.
75	    TypeBufferCommit {
76	        /// The segment whose buffer should commit.
77	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
78	    },
79	
80	    /// Begin IME composition for segment text input.
81	    CompositionStart,
82

455	impl Context {
456	    /// Recomputes segments from the current value, locale, granularity, and hour cycle.
457	    pub fn rebuild_segments(&mut self) {
458	        self.segments = build_segments(self);
459	    }
460	
461	    /// Returns the index of a segment kind in display order.
462	    #[must_use]
463	    pub fn segment_index(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) -> Option<usize> {
464	        self.segments
465	            .iter()
466	            .position(|segment| segment.kind == kind)
467	    }
468	
469	    /// Returns the first editable segment.
470	    #[must_use]
471	    pub fn first_editable(&self) -> Option<DateSegmentKind> {
472	        self.segments
473	            .iter()
474	            .find(|segment| segment.is_editable)
475	            .map(|segment| segment.kind)
476	    }
477	
478	    /// Returns the next editable segment after `kind`.
479	    #[must_use]
480	    pub fn next_editable_after(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) -> Option<DateSegmentKind> {
481	        let index = self.segment_index(kind)?;
482	
483	        self.segments[index + 1..]
484	            .iter()
485	            .find(|segment| segment.is_editable)
486	            .map(|segment| segment.kind)
487	    }
488	
489	    /// Returns the previous editable segment before `kind`.
490	    #[must_use]
491	    pub fn prev_editable_before(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) -> Option<DateSegmentKind> {
492	        let index = self.segment_index(kind)?;
493	
494	        self.segments[..index]
495	            .iter()
496	            .rev()
497	            .find(|segment| segment.is_editable)
498	            .map(|segment| segment.kind)
499	    }
500	
501	    /// Returns `true` when every visible editable time segment has a value.
502	    #[must_use]
503	    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {
504	        self.segments
505	            .iter()
506	            .filter(|segment| segment.is_editable)
507	            .all(|segment| segment.value.is_some())
508	    }
509	
510	    /// Assembles a validated time from current segment values.
511	    #[must_use]
512	    pub fn assemble_time(&self) -> Option<Time> {
513	        let raw_hour = u8::try_from(self.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Hour)?).ok()?;
514	
515	        let minute = if self.granularity >= TimeGranularity::Minute {
516	            u8::try_from(self.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Minute)?).ok()?
517	        } else {
518	            0
519	        };
520	
521	        let second = if self.granularity >= TimeGranularity::Second {
522	            u8::try_from(self.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Second)?).ok()?
523	        } else {
524	            0
525	        };
526	
527	        let day_period = self.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod);
528	
529	        let hour = display_hour_to_24(raw_hour, day_period, self.hour_cycle)?;
530	
531	        Time::new(hour, minute, second, 0).ok()
532	    }
533	
534	    /// Returns the current numeric value for a segment.
535	    #[must_use]
536	    pub fn get_segment_value(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) -> Option<i32> {
537	        self.segments
538	            .iter()
539	            .find(|segment| segment.kind == kind)?
540	            .value
541	    }
542	
543	    /// Returns a mutable reference to a segment.
544	    pub fn segment_mut(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind) -> Option<&mut DateSegment> {
545	        self.segments
546	            .iter_mut()
547	            .find(|segment| segment.kind == kind)
548	    }
549	
550	    /// Sets and formats a segment value within that segment's valid range.
551	    pub fn set_segment_value(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind, raw: i32) {
552	        let Some(index) = self
553	            .segments
554	            .iter()
555	            .position(|segment| segment.kind == kind)
556	        else {
557	            return;
558	        };
559	
560	        if !self.segments[index].is_editable {
561	            return;
562	        }
563	
564	        let min = self.segments[index].min;
565	        let max = self.segments[index].max;
566	        let value = raw.clamp(min, max);
567	        let text = self.format_segment_value(kind, value);
568	
569	        self.segments[index].value = Some(value);
570	        self.segments[index].text = text;
571	    }
572	
573	    /// Clears a segment value.
574	    pub fn clear_segment_value(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind) {
575	        if let Some(segment) = self.segment_mut(kind) {
576	            segment.value = None;
577	            segment.text.clear();
578	        }
579	    }
580	
581	    /// Increment the value of a segment, wrapping within its range.
582	    pub fn increment_segment(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind) {
583	        self.step_segment_value(kind, 1);
584	    }
585	
586	    /// Decrement the value of a segment, wrapping within its range.
587	    pub fn decrement_segment(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind) {
588	        self.step_segment_value(kind, -1);
589	    }
590	
591	    fn step_segment_value(&mut self, kind: DateSegmentKind, delta: i32) {
592	        let Some(segment) = self
593	            .segments
594	            .iter()
595	            .find(|segment| segment.kind == kind && segment.is_editable)
596	            .cloned()
597	        else {
598	            return;
599	        };
600	
601	        if kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod && segment.value.is_none() {
602	            self.set_segment_value(kind, if delta >= 0 { segment.max } else { segment.min });
603	
604	            return;
605	        }
606	
607	        let current = segment.value.unwrap_or(if delta >= 0 {
608	            segment.min - 1
609	        } else {
610	            segment.max + 1
611	        });
612	
613	        let span = segment.max - segment.min + 1;
614	        let next = (current - segment.min + delta).rem_euclid(span) + segment.min;
615	
616	        self.set_segment_value(kind, next);
617	    }
618	
619	    fn format_segment_value(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind, value: i32) -> String {
620	        format_time_segment(
621	            self.intl_backend.as_ref(),
622	            &self.locale,
623	            kind,
624	            value,
625	            self.force_leading_zeros,
626	        )
627	    }
628	}
