666	/// Machine for the `TimeField` component.
667	#[derive(Debug)]
668	pub struct Machine;
669	
670	impl ars_core::Machine for Machine {
671	    type State = State;
672	    type Event = Event;
673	    type Context = Context;
674	    type Props = Props;
675	    type Messages = Messages;
676	    type Effect = Effect;
677	    type Api<'a> = Api<'a>;
678	
679	    fn init(
680	        props: &Self::Props,
681	        env: &Env,
682	        messages: &Self::Messages,
683	    ) -> (Self::State, Self::Context) {
684	        let value = if let Some(value) = props.value {
685	            Bindable::controlled(value)
686	        } else {
687	            Bindable::uncontrolled(props.default_value)
688	        };
689	
690	        let mut ctx = Context {
691	            value,
692	            segments: Vec::new(),
693	            focused_segment: None,
694	            type_buffer: String::new(),
695	            is_composing: false,
696	            locale: env.locale.clone(),
697	            messages: messages.clone(),
698	            intl_backend: Arc::clone(&env.intl_backend),
699	            granularity: props.granularity,
700	            hour_cycle: props
701	                .hour_cycle
702	                .unwrap_or_else(|| env.locale.hour_cycle(env.intl_backend.as_ref())),
703	            disabled: props.disabled,
704	            readonly: props.readonly,
705	            min_value: props.min_value,
706	            max_value: props.max_value,
707	            ids: ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id),
708	            force_leading_zeros: props.force_leading_zeros,
709	        };
710	
711	        ctx.rebuild_segments();
712	
713	        (State::Idle, ctx)
714	    }
715	
716	    fn on_props_changed(old: &Self::Props, new: &Self::Props) -> Vec<Self::Event> {
717	        if old == new {
718	            Vec::new()
719	        } else {
720	            vec![Event::SyncProps(Box::new(new.clone()))]
721	        }
722	    }
723	
724	    fn transition(
725	        state: &Self::State,
726	        event: &Self::Event,
727	        ctx: &Self::Context,
728	        _props: &Self::Props,
729	    ) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Self>> {
730	        if ctx.disabled && !matches!(event, Event::SetValue(_) | Event::SyncProps(_)) {
731	            return None;
732	        }
733	
734	        match event {
735	            Event::SyncProps(props) => {
736	                let props = props.as_ref().clone();
737	                let mut next_ctx = ctx.clone();
738	
739	                sync_props(&mut next_ctx, &props);
740	
741	                let next_state = reconcile_state_after_sync(state, &next_ctx);
742	                let should_apply_focus = next_state != *state;
743	
744	                Some(
745	                    TransitionPlan::to(next_state.clone()).apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
746	                        sync_props(ctx, &props);
747	
748	                        if should_apply_focus {
749	                            apply_state_focus(ctx, &next_state);
750	                        }
751	                    }),
752	                )
753	            }
754	
755	            Event::FocusSegment { kind } => {
756	                if !kind.is_editable()
757	                    || !ctx
758	                        .segments
759	                        .iter()
760	                        .any(|segment| segment.kind == *kind && segment.is_editable)
761	                {
762	                    return None;
763	                }
764	
765	                let kind = *kind;
766	                Some(
767	                    TransitionPlan::to(State::Focused(kind)).apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
768	                        commit_type_buffer(ctx);
769	                        ctx.focused_segment = Some(kind);
770	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
771	                    }),
772	                )
773	            }
774	
775	            Event::BlurAll => Some(
776	                TransitionPlan::to(State::Idle)
777	                    .apply(|ctx: &mut Context| {
778	                        commit_type_buffer(ctx);
779	                        ctx.focused_segment = None;
780	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
781	                    })
782	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
783	            ),
784	
785	            Event::FocusNextSegment => {
786	                if let State::Focused(current) = state {
787	                    let current = *current;
788	                    let next = ctx.next_editable_after(current);
789	                    let target = next.map_or(State::Idle, State::Focused);
790	                    Some(
791	                        TransitionPlan::to(target)
792	                            .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
793	                                commit_type_buffer(ctx);
794	                                ctx.focused_segment = next;
795	                                ctx.type_buffer.clear();
796	                            })
797	                            .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
798	                    )
799	                } else {
800	                    let first = ctx.first_editable()?;
801	                    Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Focused(first)).apply(
802	                        move |ctx: &mut Context| {
803	                            ctx.focused_segment = Some(first);
804	                        },
805	                    ))
806	                }
807	            }
808	
809	            Event::FocusPrevSegment => {
810	                if let State::Focused(current) = state {
811	                    let previous = ctx.prev_editable_before(*current)?;
812	                    Some(
813	                        TransitionPlan::to(State::Focused(previous))
814	                            .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
815	                                commit_type_buffer(ctx);
816	                                ctx.focused_segment = Some(previous);
817	                                ctx.type_buffer.clear();
818	                            })
819	                            .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
820	                    )
821	                } else {
822	                    None
823	                }
824	            }
825	
826	            Event::IncrementSegment { kind } => {
827	                if ctx.readonly {
828	                    return None;
829	                }
830	
831	                let kind = *kind;
832	                Some(
833	                    TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
834	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
835	                        ctx.increment_segment(kind);
836	                        maybe_publish(ctx);
837	                    })
838	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
839	                )
840	            }
841	
842	            Event::DecrementSegment { kind } => {
843	                if ctx.readonly {
844	                    return None;
845	                }
846	
847	                let kind = *kind;
848	                Some(
849	                    TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
850	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
851	                        ctx.decrement_segment(kind);
852	                        maybe_publish(ctx);
853	                    })
854	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
855	                )
856	            }
857	
858	            Event::TypeIntoSegment { kind, ch } => {
859	                if ctx.readonly || ctx.is_composing || !matches!(state, State::Focused(_)) {
860	                    return None;
861	                }
862	
863	                type_into_segment(ctx, state, *kind, *ch)
864	            }
865	
866	            Event::TypeBufferCommit { kind } => {
867	                if ctx.readonly {
868	                    return None;
869	                }
870	
871	                let kind = *kind;
872	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
873	                    commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx, kind, true);
874	                }))
875	            }
876	
877	            Event::CompositionStart => Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(|ctx: &mut Context| {
878	                ctx.is_composing = true;
879	            })),
880	
881	            Event::CompositionEnd { kind, text } => {
882	                let kind = *kind;
883	                let text = text.clone();
884	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
885	                    ctx.is_composing = false;
886	
887	                    if ctx.readonly || ctx.disabled {
888	                        return;
889	                    }
890	
891	                    apply_composed_text(ctx, kind, &text);
892	                }))
893	            }
894	
895	            Event::ClearSegment { kind } => {
896	                if ctx.readonly {
897	                    return None;
898	                }
899	
900	                let kind = *kind;
901	                Some(
902	                    TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
903	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
904	                        ctx.clear_segment_value(kind);
905	                        ctx.value.set(None);
906	                    })
907	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
908	                )
909	            }
910	
911	            Event::ClearAll => {
912	                if ctx.readonly {
913	                    return None;
914	                }
915	
916	                Some(
917	                    TransitionPlan::to(State::Idle)
918	                        .apply(|ctx: &mut Context| {
919	                            let editable = ctx
920	                                .segments
921	                                .iter()
922	                                .filter(|segment| segment.is_editable)
923	                                .map(|segment| segment.kind)
924	                                .collect::<Vec<_>>();
925	
926	                            for kind in editable {
927	                                ctx.clear_segment_value(kind);
928	                            }
929	
930	                            ctx.value.set(None);
931	                            ctx.focused_segment = None;
932	                            ctx.type_buffer.clear();
933	                        })
934	                        .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
935	                )
936	            }
937	
938	            Event::SetValue(value) => {
939	                let value = *value;
940	                Some(
941	                    TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
942	                        apply_value(ctx, value);
943	                        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
944	                    })
945	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
946	                )
947	            }
948	        }
949	    }
950	
951	    fn connect<'a>(
952	        state: &'a Self::State,
953	        ctx: &'a Self::Context,
954	        props: &'a Self::Props,
955	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Self::Event),
956	    ) -> Self::Api<'a> {
957	        Api {
958	            state,
959	            ctx,
960	            props,
961	            send,
962	        }
963	    }
964	}
965	
966	/// Structural parts exposed by the `TimeField` connect API.
967	#[derive(ComponentPart)]
968	#[scope = "time-field"]
969	pub enum Part {
970	    /// The root container.
971	    Root,
972	
973	    /// The visible label.
974	    Label,
975	
976	    /// The group containing all rendered segments.
977	    FieldGroup,
978	
979	    /// An editable time segment.
980	    Segment {
981	        /// The logical kind of the segment.
982	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
983	    },
984	
985	    /// A non-editable literal separator.
986	    Literal {
987	        /// The literal's rendered index.
988	        index: usize,
989	    },
990	
991	    /// The optional description element.
992	    Description,
993	
994	    /// The optional error message element.
995	    ErrorMessage,
996	
997	    /// The hidden form input.
998	    HiddenInput,
999	}
1000	
1001	/// API for the `TimeField` component.
1002	pub struct Api<'a> {
1003	    /// The state of the component.
1004	    state: &'a State,
1005	
1006	    /// The context of the component.
1007	    ctx: &'a Context,
1008	
1009	    /// The props of the component.
1010	    props: &'a Props,
1011	
1012	    /// The send function.
1013	    send: &'a dyn Fn(Event),
1014	}
1015	
1016	impl Debug for Api<'_> {
1017	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
1018	        f.debug_struct("Api")
1019	            .field("state", self.state)
1020	            .field("ctx", self.ctx)
1021	            .field("props", self.props)
1022	            .finish_non_exhaustive()
1023	    }
1024	}

1026	impl<'a> Api<'a> {
1027	    /// Creates a `TimeField` connect API from machine state.
1028	    #[must_use]
1029	    pub const fn new(
1030	        state: &'a State,
1031	        ctx: &'a Context,
1032	        props: &'a Props,
1033	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Event),
1034	    ) -> Self {
1035	        Self {
1036	            state,
1037	            ctx,
1038	            props,
1039	            send,
1040	        }
1041	    }
1042	
1043	    /// Returns attributes for the outer root element.
1044	    #[must_use]
1045	    pub fn root_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
1046	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1047	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::Root.data_attrs();
1048	
1049	        attrs
1050	            .set(HtmlAttr::Id, self.ctx.ids.id())
1051	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
1052	            .set(part_attr, part_val)
1053	            .set(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-state"), self.state_name());
1054	
1055	        if self.ctx.disabled {
1056	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-disabled"), true);
1057	        }
1058	
1059	        if self.ctx.readonly {
1060	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-readonly"), true);
1061	        }
1062	
1063	        if self.props.invalid {
1064	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-invalid"), true);
1065	        }
1066	
1067	        attrs
1068	    }
1069	
1070	    /// Returns attributes for the visible label element.
1071	    #[must_use]
1072	    pub fn label_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
1073	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1074	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::Label.data_attrs();
1075	
1076	        attrs
1077	            .set(HtmlAttr::Id, self.ctx.ids.part("label"))
1078	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
1079	            .set(part_attr, part_val);
1080	
1081	        attrs
1082	    }
1083	
1084	    /// Returns attributes for the segment group.
1085	    #[must_use]
1086	    pub fn field_group_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
1087	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1088	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::FieldGroup.data_attrs();
1089	
1090	        attrs
1091	            .set(HtmlAttr::Id, self.ctx.ids.part("field-group"))
1092	            .set(HtmlAttr::Role, "group")
1093	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
1094	            .set(part_attr, part_val);
1095	
1096	        if let Some(label) = &self.props.aria_label {
1097	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Label), label);
1098	        } else {
1099	            attrs.set(
1100	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::LabelledBy),
1101	                self.ctx.ids.part("label"),
1102	            );
1103	        }
1104	
1105	        if self.ctx.disabled {
1106	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Disabled), "true");
1107	        }
1108	
1109	        if self.ctx.readonly {
1110	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ReadOnly), "true");
1111	        }
1112	
1113	        if self.props.invalid {
1114	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Invalid), "true");
1115	        }
1116	
1117	        if self.props.required {
1118	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Required), "true");
1119	        }
1120	
1121	        let mut described_by = Vec::new();
1122	
1123	        if self.props.description.is_some() {
1124	            described_by.push(self.ctx.ids.part("description"));
1125	        }
1126	
1127	        if self.props.invalid && self.props.error_message.is_some() {
1128	            described_by.push(self.ctx.ids.part("error-message"));
1129	        }
1130	
1131	        if let Some(extra) = &self.props.aria_describedby {
1132	            described_by.push(extra.clone());
1133	        }
1134	
1135	        if !described_by.is_empty() {
1136	            attrs.set(
1137	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::DescribedBy),
1138	                described_by.join(" "),
1139	            );
1140	        }
1141	
1142	        attrs
1143	    }
1144	
1145	    /// Returns attributes for an editable segment or a literal fallback.
1146	    #[must_use]
1147	    pub fn segment_attrs(&self, kind: &DateSegmentKind) -> AttrMap {
1148	        let Some((index, segment)) = self
1149	            .ctx
1150	            .segments
1151	            .iter()
1152	            .enumerate()
1153	            .find(|(_, segment)| segment.kind == *kind)
1154	        else {
1155	            return AttrMap::new();
1156	        };
1157	
1158	        if !segment.is_editable {
1159	            return self.literal_attrs(index);
1160	        }
1161	
1162	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1163	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] =
1164	            (Part::Segment { kind: *kind }).data_attrs();
1165	
1166	        let is_focused = self.ctx.focused_segment == Some(segment.kind);
1167	
1168	        let is_tab_stop = !self.ctx.disabled
1169	            && (is_focused
1170	                || (self.ctx.focused_segment.is_none()
1171	                    && self.ctx.first_editable() == Some(segment.kind)));
1172	
1173	        attrs
1174	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
1175	            .set(part_attr, part_val)
1176	            .set(HtmlAttr::Role, "spinbutton")
1177	            .set(
1178	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Label),
1179	                time_segment_aria_label(segment.kind, &self.ctx.messages, &self.ctx.locale),
1180	            )
1181	            .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ValueMin), segment.min.to_string())
1182	            .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ValueMax), segment.max.to_string())
1183	            .set(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-segment"), segment.kind.data_name())
1184	            .set(HtmlAttr::TabIndex, if is_tab_stop { "0" } else { "-1" });
1185	
1186	        if let Some(value) = segment.value {
1187	            attrs
1188	                .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ValueNow), value.to_string())
1189	                .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ValueText), &segment.text);
1190	        } else {
1191	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ValueText), &segment.placeholder);
1192	        }
1193	
1194	        if self.ctx.disabled {
1195	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Disabled), "true");
1196	        }
1197	
1198	        if self.ctx.readonly {
1199	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ReadOnly), "true");
1200	        }
1201	
1202	        if self.props.invalid && self.props.error_message.is_some() {
1203	            attrs.set(
1204	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::DescribedBy),
1205	                self.ctx.ids.part("error-message"),
1206	            );
1207	        }
1208	
1209	        attrs
1210	    }
1211	
1212	    /// Returns attributes for a literal separator.
1213	    #[must_use]
1214	    pub fn literal_attrs(&self, index: usize) -> AttrMap {
1215	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1216	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] =
1217	            (Part::Literal { index }).data_attrs();
1218	
1219	        attrs
1220	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
1221	            .set(part_attr, part_val)
1222	            .set(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-index"), index.to_string())
1223	            .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Hidden), "true")
1224	            .set(HtmlAttr::TabIndex, "-1");
1225	
1226	        attrs
1227	    }

1252	            .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Live), "polite")

1259	    /// Returns attributes for the hidden form input.
1260	    #[must_use]
1261	    pub fn hidden_input_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
1262	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
1263	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::HiddenInput.data_attrs();
1264	
1265	        let value = self
1266	            .ctx
1267	            .value
1268	            .get()
1269	            .map(|time| time.to_iso8601())
1270	            .unwrap_or_default();

1272	        attrs

1285	    /// Handles focusout on the field group.
1286	    pub fn on_field_group_focusout(&self, focus_leaving_group: bool) {
1287	        if focus_leaving_group {
1288	            (self.send)(Event::BlurAll);

1292	    /// Handles keydown on a segment.
1293	    pub fn on_segment_keydown(
1294	        &self,
1295	        kind: DateSegmentKind,
1296	        data: &KeyboardEventData,
1297	        shift: bool,
1298	        dir: Direction,
1299	    ) {
1300	        if data.is_composing {
1301	            (self.send)(Event::CompositionStart);
1302	
1303	            return;
1304	        }
1305	
1306	        let is_rtl = dir.is_rtl();
1307	
1308	        match data.key {
1309	            KeyboardKey::ArrowUp => (self.send)(Event::IncrementSegment { kind }),
1310	
1311	            KeyboardKey::ArrowDown => (self.send)(Event::DecrementSegment { kind }),
1312	
1313	            KeyboardKey::ArrowRight if is_rtl => (self.send)(Event::FocusPrevSegment),
1314	
1315	            KeyboardKey::ArrowLeft if is_rtl => (self.send)(Event::FocusNextSegment),
1316	
1317	            KeyboardKey::ArrowLeft => (self.send)(Event::FocusPrevSegment),
1318	
1319	            KeyboardKey::Tab if shift => (self.send)(Event::FocusPrevSegment),
1320	
1321	            KeyboardKey::ArrowRight | KeyboardKey::Tab => (self.send)(Event::FocusNextSegment),
1322	
1323	            KeyboardKey::Backspace | KeyboardKey::Delete => {
1324	                (self.send)(Event::ClearSegment { kind });
1325	            }
1326	
1327	            KeyboardKey::Escape => (self.send)(Event::ClearAll),
1328	
1329	            _ if data.character.is_some() => {
1330	                if let Some(ch) = data.character {
1331	                    (self.send)(Event::TypeIntoSegment { kind, ch });
1332	                }
1333	            }
1334	
1335	            _ => {}
1336	        }
1337	    }
1338	
1339	    /// Handles focus on a segment.
1340	    pub fn on_segment_focus(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) {
1341	        (self.send)(Event::FocusSegment { kind });
1342	    }
1343	
1344	    /// Handles click on a segment.
1345	    pub fn on_segment_click(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind) {
1346	        (self.send)(Event::FocusSegment { kind });
1347	    }
1348	
1349	    /// Handles composition start on a segment.
1350	    pub fn on_segment_composition_start(&self) {
1351	        (self.send)(Event::CompositionStart);
1352	    }
1353	
1354	    /// Handles composition end on a segment.
1355	    pub fn on_segment_composition_end(&self, kind: DateSegmentKind, text: impl Into<String>) {
1356	        (self.send)(Event::CompositionEnd {
1357	            kind,
1358	            text: text.into(),
1359	        });
1360	    }

1362	    /// Returns the rendered segments.
1363	    #[must_use]
1364	    pub fn segments(&self) -> &[DateSegment] {
1365	        &self.ctx.segments
1366	    }
1367	
1368	    /// Returns the current time value.
1369	    #[must_use]
1370	    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&Time> {
1371	        self.ctx.value.get().as_ref()
1372	    }
1373	
1374	    /// Returns `true` when any segment has focus.
1375	    #[must_use]
1376	    pub const fn is_focused(&self) -> bool {
1377	        !matches!(self.state, State::Idle)
1378	    }
1379	
1380	    const fn state_name(&self) -> &'static str {
1381	        match self.state {
1382	            State::Idle => "idle",
1383	            State::Focused(_) => "focused",
1384	        }
1385	    }
1386	}

1388	impl ConnectApi for Api<'_> {
1389	    type Part = Part;
1390	
1391	    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap {
1392	        match part {
1393	            Part::Root => self.root_attrs(),
1394	            Part::Label => self.label_attrs(),
1395	            Part::FieldGroup => self.field_group_attrs(),
1396	            Part::Segment { kind } => self.segment_attrs(&kind),
1397	            Part::Literal { index } => self.literal_attrs(index),
1398	            Part::Description => self.description_attrs(),
1399	            Part::ErrorMessage => self.error_message_attrs(),
1400	            Part::HiddenInput => self.hidden_input_attrs(),
1401	        }
1402	    }
1403	}
1404	
1405	fn type_into_segment(
1406	    ctx: &Context,
1407	    state: &State,
1408	    kind: DateSegmentKind,
1409	    ch: char,
1410	) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Machine>> {
1411	    if kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod {
1412	        let mut buffer = ctx.type_buffer.clone();
1413	
1414	        buffer.push(ch);
1415	
1416	        let has_cjk_table = cjk_day_period_table(&ctx.locale).is_some();
1417	
1418	        let cjk_value = has_cjk_table
1419	            .then(|| day_period_from_cjk_buffer(&buffer, &ctx.locale, ctx.hour_cycle, None, None))
1420	            .flatten();
1421	
1422	        let backend_value = ctx
1423	            .intl_backend
1424	            .day_period_from_char(ch, &ctx.locale)
1425	            .map(|is_pm| if is_pm { 1 } else { 0 });
1426	
1427	        if has_cjk_table
1428	            && cjk_value.is_none()
1429	            && backend_value.is_none()
1430	            && is_cjk_day_period_prefix(&buffer, &ctx.locale)
1431	        {
1432	            return Some(
1433	                TransitionPlan::to(state.clone())
1434	                    .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
1435	                        ctx.type_buffer = buffer;
1436	                    })
1437	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit)
1438	                    .with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::TypeBufferCommit)),
1439	            );
1440	        }
1441	
1442	        let value = cjk_value.or(backend_value)?;
1443	
1444	        let next = ctx.next_editable_after(kind);
1445	
1446	        let target = next.map_or_else(|| state.clone(), State::Focused);
1447	
1448	        return Some(
1449	            TransitionPlan::to(target)
1450	                .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
1451	                    ctx.set_segment_value(kind, value);
1452	
1453	                    ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1454	
1455	                    if let Some(next) = next {
1456	                        ctx.focused_segment = Some(next);
1457	                    }
1458	
1459	                    maybe_publish(ctx);
1460	                })
1461	                .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit),
1462	        );
1463	    }
1464	
1465	    if !matches!(
1466	        kind,
1467	        DateSegmentKind::Hour | DateSegmentKind::Minute | DateSegmentKind::Second
1468	    ) {
1469	        return None;
1470	    }
1471	
1472	    if !ch.is_ascii_digit() {
1473	        return None;
1474	    }
1475	
1476	    let mut new_buffer = ctx.type_buffer.clone();
1477	
1478	    new_buffer.push(ch);
1479	
1480	    let buffered = new_buffer.parse::<i32>().ok()?;
1481	
1482	    let segment = ctx.segments.iter().find(|segment| segment.kind == kind)?;
1483	
1484	    let max_digits = digits_needed(u32::try_from(segment.max).ok()?);
1485	
1486	    let should_advance = new_buffer.len() >= max_digits || buffered * 10 > segment.max;
1487	
1488	    let next = should_advance
1489	        .then(|| ctx.next_editable_after(kind))
1490	        .flatten();
1491	
1492	    let target = if should_advance {
1493	        next.map_or_else(|| state.clone(), State::Focused)
1494	    } else {
1495	        state.clone()
1496	    };
1497	
1498	    let mut plan = TransitionPlan::to(target).apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
1499	        ctx.type_buffer.push(ch);
1500	        let Some(segment) = ctx.segments.iter().find(|segment| segment.kind == kind) else {
1501	            return;
1502	        };
1503	
1504	        if let Ok(value) = ctx.type_buffer.parse::<i32>()
1505	            && value >= segment.min
1506	            && value <= segment.max
1507	        {
1508	            ctx.set_segment_value(kind, value);
1509	
1510	            maybe_publish(ctx);
1511	        }
1512	
1513	        if should_advance {
1514	            ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1515	
1516	            if let Some(next) = next {
1517	                ctx.focused_segment = Some(next);
1518	            }
1519	        }
1520	    });
1521	
1522	    if should_advance {
1523	        plan = plan.cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit);
1524	    } else {
1525	        plan = plan
1526	            .cancel_effect(Effect::TypeBufferCommit)
1527	            .with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::TypeBufferCommit));
1528	    }
1529	
1530	    Some(plan)
1531	}
1532	
1533	fn commit_type_buffer(ctx: &mut Context) {
1534	    if let Some(kind) = ctx.focused_segment {
1535	        commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx, kind, kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod);
1536	    }
1537	}
1538	
1539	fn commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx: &mut Context, kind: DateSegmentKind, timeout_fallback: bool) {
1540	    if kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod {
1541	        if ctx.type_buffer.is_empty() {
1542	            return;
1543	        }
1544	
1545	        let current_hour = timeout_fallback
1546	            .then(|| {
1547	                ctx.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Hour)
1548	                    .and_then(|value| u8::try_from(value).ok())
1549	            })
1550	            .flatten();
1551	        let current_day_period = timeout_fallback
1552	            .then(|| ctx.get_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod))
1553	            .flatten();
1554	
1555	        if let Some(value) = day_period_from_cjk_buffer(
1556	            &ctx.type_buffer,
1557	            &ctx.locale,
1558	            ctx.hour_cycle,
1559	            current_hour,
1560	            current_day_period,
1561	        ) {
1562	            ctx.set_segment_value(kind, value);
1563	
1564	            maybe_publish(ctx);
1565	        }
1566	
1567	        ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1568	
1569	        return;
1570	    }
1571	
1572	    if let Ok(value) = ctx.type_buffer.parse::<i32>()
1573	        && let Some(segment) = ctx.segments.iter().find(|segment| segment.kind == kind)
1574	        && value >= segment.min
1575	        && value <= segment.max
1576	    {
1577	        ctx.set_segment_value(kind, value);
1578	
1579	        maybe_publish(ctx);
1580	    }
1581	
1582	    ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1583	}
1584	
1585	fn apply_composed_text(ctx: &mut Context, kind: DateSegmentKind, text: &str) {
1586	    ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1587	
1588	    if kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod {
1589	        if let Some(value) = text.chars().find_map(|ch| {
1590	            ctx.intl_backend
1591	                .day_period_from_char(ch, &ctx.locale)
1592	                .map(|is_pm| if is_pm { 1 } else { 0 })
1593	        }) {
1594	            ctx.set_segment_value(kind, value);
1595	            maybe_publish(ctx);
1596	
1597	            return;
1598	        }
1599	
1600	        ctx.type_buffer.push_str(text);
1601	        commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx, kind, true);
1602	
1603	        return;
1604	    }
1605	
1606	    if matches!(
1607	        kind,
1608	        DateSegmentKind::Hour | DateSegmentKind::Minute | DateSegmentKind::Second
1609	    ) {
1610	        ctx.type_buffer
1611	            .extend(text.chars().filter(char::is_ascii_digit));
1612	        commit_buffer_for_kind(ctx, kind, false);
1613	    }
1614	}
1615	
1616	fn maybe_publish(ctx: &mut Context) {
1617	    if !ctx.is_complete() {
1618	        return;
1619	    }
1620	
1621	    let Some(time) = ctx.assemble_time() else {
1622	        return;
1623	    };
1624	
1625	    let clamped = clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref());
1626	
1627	    apply_segments_from_time(ctx, clamped);
1628	
1629	    ctx.value.set(Some(clamped));
1630	}
1631	
1632	fn apply_value(ctx: &mut Context, value: Option<Time>) {
1633	    let value = value.map(|time| clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref()));
1634	
1635	    if ctx.value.is_controlled() {
1636	        ctx.value.set(value);
1637	        ctx.value.sync_controlled(Some(value));
1638	    } else {
1639	        ctx.value.set(value);
1640	    }
1641	
1642	    ctx.rebuild_segments();
1643	}
1644	
1645	fn apply_controlled_value(ctx: &mut Context, value: Option<Time>) {
1646	    let value = value.map(|time| clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref()));
1647	
1648	    ctx.value.set(value);
1649	    ctx.value.sync_controlled(Some(value));
1650	    ctx.rebuild_segments();
1651	}
1652	
1653	fn sync_props(ctx: &mut Context, props: &Props) {
1654	    let was_controlled = ctx.value.is_controlled();
1655	    let previous_granularity = ctx.granularity;
1656	    let previous_hour_cycle = ctx.hour_cycle;
1657	    let previous_force_leading_zeros = ctx.force_leading_zeros;
1658	
1659	    ctx.granularity = props.granularity;
1660	    ctx.hour_cycle = props
1661	        .hour_cycle
1662	        .unwrap_or_else(|| ctx.locale.hour_cycle(ctx.intl_backend.as_ref()));
1663	    ctx.disabled = props.disabled;
1664	    ctx.readonly = props.readonly;
1665	    ctx.min_value = props.min_value;
1666	    ctx.max_value = props.max_value;
1667	    ctx.ids = ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id);
1668	    ctx.force_leading_zeros = props.force_leading_zeros;
1669	
1670	    if let Some(value) = props.value {
1671	        apply_controlled_value(ctx, value);
1672	    } else if was_controlled {
1673	        ctx.value.sync_controlled(None);
1674	        ctx.value.set(None);
1675	        ctx.rebuild_segments();
1676	    } else {
1677	        ctx.value.sync_controlled(None);
1678	
1679	        let clamped = ctx
1680	            .value
1681	            .get()
1682	            .map(|time| clamp_time(time, ctx.min_value.as_ref(), ctx.max_value.as_ref()));
1683	        let value_changed = clamped != *ctx.value.get();
1684	        let must_rebuild = previous_granularity != ctx.granularity
1685	            || previous_hour_cycle != ctx.hour_cycle
1686	            || previous_force_leading_zeros != ctx.force_leading_zeros
1687	            || value_changed;
1688	
1689	        ctx.value.set(clamped);
1690	
1691	        if must_rebuild {
1692	            ctx.rebuild_segments();
1693	        }
1694	    }
1695	}
1696	
1697	fn reconcile_state_after_sync(state: &State, ctx: &Context) -> State {
1698	    let State::Focused(kind) = state else {
1699	        return State::Idle;
1700	    };
1701	
1702	    let still_focusable = !ctx.disabled
1703	        && ctx
1704	            .segments
1705	            .iter()
1706	            .any(|segment| segment.kind == *kind && segment.is_editable);
1707	
1708	    if still_focusable {
1709	        State::Focused(*kind)
1710	    } else {
1711	        State::Idle
1712	    }
1713	}
1714	
1715	fn apply_state_focus(ctx: &mut Context, state: &State) {
1716	    match state {
1717	        State::Idle => {
1718	            ctx.focused_segment = None;
1719	            ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1720	        }
1721	
1722	        State::Focused(kind) => {
1723	            ctx.focused_segment = Some(*kind);
1724	            ctx.type_buffer.clear();
1725	        }
1726	    }
1727	}
1728	
1729	fn apply_segments_from_time(ctx: &mut Context, time: Time) {
1730	    for segment in &mut ctx.segments {
1731	        let value = match segment.kind {
1732	            DateSegmentKind::Hour => Some(display_hour(time, ctx.hour_cycle)),
1733	            DateSegmentKind::Minute => Some(i32::from(time.minute())),
1734	            DateSegmentKind::Second => Some(i32::from(time.second())),
1735	            DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod => Some(if is_pm(time) { 1 } else { 0 }),
1736	            _ => None,
1737	        };
1738	
1739	        if let Some(value) = value {
1740	            segment.value = Some(value);
1741	
1742	            segment.text = format_time_segment(
1743	                ctx.intl_backend.as_ref(),
1744	                &ctx.locale,
1745	                segment.kind,
1746	                value,
1747	                ctx.force_leading_zeros,
1748	            );
1749	        }
1750	    }
1751	}
1752	
1753	fn build_segments(ctx: &Context) -> Vec<DateSegment> {
1754	    let mut segments = Vec::new();
1755	
1756	    let value = *ctx.value.get();
1757	
1758	    let (hour_min, hour_max) = display_hour_range(ctx.hour_cycle);
1759	
1760	    let mut hour = DateSegment::new_numeric(DateSegmentKind::Hour, hour_min, hour_max, "hh");
1761	
1762	    if let Some(time) = value {
1763	        let display = display_hour(time, ctx.hour_cycle);
1764	
1765	        hour.value = Some(display);
1766	        hour.text = ctx.format_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Hour, display);
1767	    }
1768	
1769	    segments.push(hour);
1770	
1771	    if ctx.granularity >= TimeGranularity::Minute {
1772	        segments.push(DateSegment::new_literal(":"));
1773	
1774	        let mut minute = DateSegment::new_numeric(DateSegmentKind::Minute, 0, 59, "mm");
1775	
1776	        if let Some(time) = value {
1777	            let value = i32::from(time.minute());
1778	
1779	            minute.value = Some(value);
1780	            minute.text = ctx.format_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Minute, value);
1781	        }
1782	
1783	        segments.push(minute);
1784	    }
1785	
1786	    if ctx.granularity >= TimeGranularity::Second {
1787	        segments.push(DateSegment::new_literal(":"));
1788	
1789	        let mut second = DateSegment::new_numeric(DateSegmentKind::Second, 0, 59, "ss");
1790	
1791	        if let Some(time) = value {
1792	            let value = i32::from(time.second());
1793	
1794	            second.value = Some(value);
1795	            second.text = ctx.format_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::Second, value);
1796	        }
1797	
1798	        segments.push(second);
1799	    }
1800	
1801	    if has_day_period(ctx.hour_cycle) {
1802	        segments.push(DateSegment::new_literal("\u{00A0}"));
1803	
1804	        let mut period = DateSegment {
1805	            kind: DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod,
1806	            value: None,
1807	            min: 0,
1808	            max: 1,
1809	            text: String::new(),
1810	            placeholder: ctx.intl_backend.day_period_label(false, &ctx.locale),
1811	            literal: None,
1812	            is_editable: true,
1813	        };
1814	
1815	        if let Some(time) = value {
1816	            let value = if is_pm(time) { 1 } else { 0 };
1817	
1818	            period.value = Some(value);
1819	            period.text = ctx.format_segment_value(DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod, value);
1820	        }
1821	
1822	        segments.push(period);
1823	    }
1824	
1825	    segments
1826	}
1827	
1828	const fn display_hour_range(hour_cycle: HourCycle) -> (i32, i32) {
1829	    match hour_cycle {
1830	        HourCycle::H11 => (0, 11),
1831	        HourCycle::H12 => (1, 12),
1832	        HourCycle::H23 => (0, 23),
1833	        HourCycle::H24 => (1, 24),
1834	    }
1835	}
1836	
1837	const fn has_day_period(hour_cycle: HourCycle) -> bool {
1838	    matches!(hour_cycle, HourCycle::H11 | HourCycle::H12)
1839	}
1840	
1841	fn display_hour(time: Time, hour_cycle: HourCycle) -> i32 {
1842	    let hour = time.hour();
1843	
1844	    match hour_cycle {
1845	        HourCycle::H11 => i32::from(hour % 12),
1846	
1847	        HourCycle::H12 => i32::from(if hour.is_multiple_of(12) {
1848	            12
1849	        } else {
1850	            hour % 12
1851	        }),
1852	
1853	        HourCycle::H23 => i32::from(hour),
1854	
1855	        HourCycle::H24 => i32::from(if hour == 0 { 24 } else { hour }),
1856	    }
1857	}
1858	
1859	fn display_hour_to_24(raw_hour: u8, day_period: Option<i32>, hour_cycle: HourCycle) -> Option<u8> {
1860	    match hour_cycle {
1861	        HourCycle::H11 => {
1862	            let is_pm = day_period.unwrap_or(0) == 1;
1863	            Some(if is_pm { raw_hour + 12 } else { raw_hour })
1864	        }
1865	
1866	        HourCycle::H12 => {
1867	            let is_pm = day_period.unwrap_or(0) == 1;
1868	            Some(match (raw_hour, is_pm) {
1869	                (12, false) => 0,
1870	                (12, true) => 12,
1871	                (_, true) => raw_hour + 12,
1872	                (_, false) => raw_hour,
1873	            })
1874	        }
1875	
1876	        HourCycle::H23 => Some(raw_hour),
1877	
1878	        HourCycle::H24 => Some(if raw_hour == 24 { 0 } else { raw_hour }),
1879	    }
1880	}
1881	
1882	const fn is_pm(time: Time) -> bool {
1883	    time.hour() >= 12
1884	}
1885	
1886	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
1887	struct CjkDayPeriodEntry {
1888	    am_label: &'static str,
1889	    pm_label: &'static str,
1890	}
1891	
1892	fn cjk_day_period_table(locale: &Locale) -> Option<CjkDayPeriodEntry> {
1893	    match locale.language() {
1894	        "ja" => Some(CjkDayPeriodEntry {
1895	            am_label: "午前",
1896	            pm_label: "午後",
1897	        }),
1898	
1899	        "ko" => Some(CjkDayPeriodEntry {
1900	            am_label: "오전",
1901	            pm_label: "오후",
1902	        }),
1903	
1904	        _ => None,
1905	    }
1906	}
1907	
1908	fn is_cjk_day_period_prefix(buffer: &str, locale: &Locale) -> bool {
1909	    let Some(entry) = cjk_day_period_table(locale) else {
1910	        return false;
1911	    };
1912	
1913	    let normalized = strip_combining_marks(buffer);
1914	
1915	    if normalized.is_empty() {
1916	        return false;
1917	    }
1918	
1919	    entry.am_label.starts_with(&normalized) || entry.pm_label.starts_with(&normalized)
1920	}
1921	
1922	fn day_period_from_cjk_buffer(
1923	    buffer: &str,
1924	    locale: &Locale,
1925	    hour_cycle: HourCycle,
1926	    current_hour: Option<u8>,
1927	    current_day_period: Option<i32>,
1928	) -> Option<i32> {
1929	    let entry = cjk_day_period_table(locale)?;
1930	
1931	    let normalized = strip_combining_marks(buffer);
1932	
1933	    if normalized.is_empty() {
1934	        return None;
1935	    }
1936	
1937	    let am_matches = entry.am_label.starts_with(&normalized);
1938	    let pm_matches = entry.pm_label.starts_with(&normalized);
1939	
1940	    match (
1941	        am_matches,
1942	        pm_matches,
1943	        hour_cycle,
1944	        current_hour,
1945	        current_day_period,
1946	    ) {
1947	        (true, false, ..) => Some(0),
1948	        (false, true, ..) => Some(1),
1949	        (true, true, _, _, Some(day_period)) => Some(day_period.clamp(0, 1)),
1950	        (true, true, HourCycle::H11, _, None) | (true, true, _, None, _) | (false, false, ..) => {
1951	            None
1952	        }
1953	        (true, true, _, Some(hour), _) => Some(if hour < 12 { 0 } else { 1 }),
1954	    }
1955	}
1956	
1957	fn strip_combining_marks(input: &str) -> String {
1958	    input
1959	        .chars()
1960	        .filter(|ch| {
1961	            !matches!(
1962	                u32::from(*ch),
1963	                0x0300..=0x036F
1964	                    | 0x1AB0..=0x1AFF
1965	                    | 0x1DC0..=0x1DFF
1966	                    | 0x20D0..=0x20FF
1967	                    | 0xFE20..=0xFE2F
1968	            )
1969	        })
1970	        .collect()
1971	}
1972	
1973	fn format_time_segment(
1974	    backend: &dyn IntlBackend,
1975	    locale: &Locale,
1976	    kind: DateSegmentKind,
1977	    value: i32,
1978	    force_leading_zeros: bool,
1979	) -> String {
1980	    if kind == DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod {
1981	        return backend.day_period_label(value == 1, locale);
1982	    }
1983	
1984	    let min_digits = if force_leading_zeros { 2 } else { 1 };
1985	
1986	    u32::try_from(value).map_or_else(
1987	        |_| value.to_string(),
1988	        |value| {
1989	            backend.format_segment_digits(
1990	                value,
1991	                NonZeroU8::new(min_digits).expect("segment width is non-zero"),
1992	                locale,
1993	            )
1994	        },
1995	    )
1996	}
1997	
1998	fn clamp_time(time: Time, min_value: Option<&Time>, max_value: Option<&Time>) -> Time {
1999	    if let Some(min) = min_value
2000	        && time < *min
2001	    {
2002	        return *min;
2003	    }
2004	
2005	    if let Some(max) = max_value
2006	        && time > *max
2007	    {
2008	        return *max;
2009	    }
2010	
2011	    time
2012	}
2013	
2014	const fn digits_needed(max: u32) -> usize {
2015	    match max {
2016	        0..=9 => 1,
2017	        10..=99 => 2,
2018	        100..=999 => 3,
2019	        _ => 4,
2020	    }
2021	}
2022	
2023	fn time_segment_aria_label(kind: DateSegmentKind, messages: &Messages, locale: &Locale) -> String {
2024	    fn call(
2025	        message: &MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
2026	        locale: &Locale,
2027	    ) -> String {
2028	        message(locale)
2029	    }
2030	
2031	    match kind {
2032	        DateSegmentKind::Hour => call(&messages.hour_label, locale),
2033	        DateSegmentKind::Minute => call(&messages.minute_label, locale),
2034	        DateSegmentKind::Second => call(&messages.second_label, locale),
2035	        DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod => call(&messages.day_period_label, locale),
2036	        _ => String::new(),
2037	    }
2038	}
2039

104	
105	/// A single segment within a `DateField`.
106	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
107	pub struct DateSegment {
108	    /// The kind of segment.
109	    pub kind: DateSegmentKind,
110	
111	    /// Current numeric value, or `None` when the user has not entered it.
112	    pub value: Option<i32>,
113	
114	    /// Minimum valid numeric value for editable segments.
115	    pub min: i32,
116	
117	    /// Maximum valid numeric value for editable segments.
118	    pub max: i32,
119	
120	    /// Formatted display text for the current value.
121	    pub text: String,
122	
123	    /// Placeholder shown when the segment has no value.
124	    pub placeholder: String,
125	
126	    /// Literal text for non-editable separator segments.
127	    pub literal: Option<String>,
128	
129	    /// Whether this segment accepts keyboard input.
130	    pub is_editable: bool,
131	}
132	
133	impl DateSegment {
134	    /// Creates a new editable numeric segment.
135	    #[must_use]
136	    pub fn new_numeric(kind: DateSegmentKind, min: i32, max: i32, placeholder: &str) -> Self {
137	        Self {
138	            kind,
139	            value: None,
140	            min,
141	            max,
142	            text: String::new(),
143	            placeholder: placeholder.to_string(),
144	            literal: None,
145	            is_editable: true,
146	        }
147	    }
148	
149	    /// Creates a new non-editable literal segment.
150	    #[must_use]
151	    pub fn new_literal(ch: &str) -> Self {
152	        Self {
153	            kind: DateSegmentKind::Literal,
154	            value: None,
155	            min: 0,
156	            max: 0,
157	            text: ch.to_string(),
158	            placeholder: ch.to_string(),
159	            literal: Some(ch.to_string()),
160	            is_editable: false,
161	        }
162	    }
163	
164	    /// Creates a new non-editable semantic segment.
165	    #[must_use]
166	    pub fn new_non_editable(kind: DateSegmentKind, text: &str) -> Self {
167	        Self {
168	            kind,
169	            value: None,
170	            min: 0,
171	            max: 0,
172	            text: text.to_string(),
173	            placeholder: text.to_string(),
174	            literal: Some(text.to_string()),
175	            is_editable: false,
176	        }
177	    }
178	
179	    /// Returns the text adapters should render for this segment.
180	    #[must_use]
181	    pub fn display_text(&self) -> &str {
182	        if self.value.is_some() && !self.text.is_empty() {
183	            &self.text
184	        } else {
185	            &self.placeholder
186	        }
187	    }
188	
189	    /// Returns a human-readable ARIA value text for the current segment value.
190	    #[must_use]
191	    pub fn aria_value_text(&self, backend: &dyn IntlBackend, locale: &Locale) -> Option<String> {
192	        let value = self.value?;
193	
194	        match self.kind {
195	            DateSegmentKind::Month => u8::try_from(value)
196	                .ok()
197	                .map(|month| backend.month_long_name(month, locale)),
198	
199	            DateSegmentKind::DayPeriod => Some(backend.day_period_label(value == 1, locale)),
200	
201	            _ => Some(value.to_string()),
202	        }
203	    }
204	}

177	    pub aria_label: Option<String>,

181

    name: Unit Tests

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

content.text: Now the spec sync. First, add the reconciliation note after the intro (mirroring date-picker's style):

input.old_string:
`DateTimePicker` is a composite component that combines a `DatePicker` (segmented date input with calendar popover) and a `TimeField` (segmented time input) into a single control for selecting both date and time. The date segments and time segments render in a unified control area, separated by a visual separator. The calendar popover handles date selection; time segments are edited inline via the spinbutton pattern.

## 1. State Machine
input.new_string:
`DateTimePicker` is a composite component that combines a `DatePicker` (segmented date input with calendar popover) and a `TimeField` (segmented time input) into a single control for selecting both date and time. The date segments and time segments render in a unified control area, separated by a visual separator. The calendar popover handles date selection; time segments are edited inline via the spinbutton pattern.

> **Spec-vs-implementation reconciliation.** The code blocks below describe the
> agnostic implementation at `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_time_picker/`,
> which is the authoritative contract. Key conventions, shared with the sibling
> `date_picker`/`time_field` machines:
>
> - **Combined value is `ars_i18n::CalendarDateTime`** (`CalendarDateTime::new(date, time)`,
>   accessors `.date()`/`.time()`). It has no `Ord`, so range checks compare the
>   date via `CalendarDate::compare` and then the `Time` via `Ord`.
> - **Focus is adapter-driven via named effects.** The machine declares a typed
>   `Effect` enum and emits [`PendingEffect::named`] intents (`FocusCalendar`,
>   `RestoreFocusToTrigger`, `FocusFirstTimeSegment`, `TypeBufferCommit`); the
>   adapter performs the live, element-handle-based focus / timer. The core never
>   calls `use_platform_effects()`/`focus_element_by_id` (matches the element/ref
>   handling note in issue #292).
> - **Controlled-prop sync.** `on_props_changed` emits `Event::SyncProps(Box<Props>)`,
>   which reconciles the controlled `value` and cached scalar fields and flows
>   through even when `disabled` so a parent can lift the disabled state.
> - **Segment helpers are shared.** Hour-cycle math (`display_hour`,
>   `display_hour_to_24`, `display_hour_range`, `has_day_period`, `is_pm`) and the
>   `digits_needed` type-ahead helper live in `date_time::hour_cycle`, shared with
>   `time_field`. Date segment order comes from `ars_i18n::date_order`. Segment
>   ARIA labels resolve from the per-segment fields on this component's `Messages`.
> - **Real `CalendarDate`/`Time` APIs.** `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(year: i32,
>   month: u8, day: u8)` and `Time::new(h, m, s, millis)` are fallible; segment
>   values pass through `.ok()`. Segment kinds expose `data_name()` (not `as_str()`).

## 1. State Machine

content.text: Now §1.5 Guards — reflect that guards are inline checks:

input.new_string:
### 1.5 Guards

Guards are inline field checks inside `transition`, not separate functions:

- `ctx.disabled` blocks every event except `SyncProps` (which flows through so a
  parent can lift the disabled state).
- `ctx.readonly` blocks the editing/open paths (`Open`, `CalendarSelectDate`,
  `Increment/DecrementSegment`, `TypeIntoSegment`, `SegmentChange`, `ValueCommit`,
  `ClearSegment`, `ClearAll`) while still allowing focus navigation.

lines: +Guards are inline field checks inside `transition`, not separate functions:
+- `ctx.disabled` blocks every event except `SyncProps` (which flows through so a
+  parent can lift the disabled state).

The DateTimePicker does not embed separate `DatePicker` and `TimeField` machine instances. Instead, it manages a single unified state machine that owns both date segments and time segments. This design avoids cross-machine synchronization complexity and provides seamless segment navigation across the date-to-time boundary.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-292/.claude/skills/post-implementation-audit

# Post-implementation audit

This skill is the bridge between *"the named tests pass"* and *"the user reviews the diff"*. It exists because the initial implementation of any task is usually correct on its surface acceptance criteria but quietly drifts from the spec, leaves untested defensive code, ships APIs that diverge from convention, or carries a fold-vs-lowercase mistake that won't surface until production. Running these audits before user-review collapses three reviewer round-trips into one.

The skill is mandatory per CLAUDE.md "Development Workflow" step 7 — run it after implementing any user-requested task (feature, bug fix, refactor) and before presenting the final result for user review.

## Quick flow

The audit is strictly linear — no branching, just a loop in Phase 2:

```
Phase 1 (spec/impl drift audit)
   └─→ land all Phase 1 findings
        └─→ Phase 2 (iterative "anything missing?")
              ├─→ Round 1: surface findings → land all
              ├─→ Round 2: surface findings → land all
              └─→ keep looping until 2 consecutive clean rounds (minimum 2 rounds total)
                   └─→ Phase 3 (test coverage)
                        └─→ land all Phase 3 findings
                             └─→ Final verification suite
                                  └─→ Hand off to user (CLAUDE.md step 8)
```

The body below walks through each phase in order. Read top-to-bottom; the structure mirrors the execution order.

## Operating principle: no deferral

**Every recommendation each audit phase produces lands in *this* PR.** Don't open follow-up issues. Don't TODO-flag. Don't promise a future PR or a future sprint. Don't quietly skip a finding because it feels like scope creep.

If a finding is genuinely out of scope (e.g., requires a workspace-wide refactor touching 50+ files), surface it explicitly to the user with that reasoning visible — but the default is to land everything. This constraint is what makes the audit valuable: a deferred finding is a guarantee that the original implementation's bias survives into `main`, where it costs ten times as much to undo.

The user will tell you "land it all". Anticipate that, and just do it.

## Three sequential phases

Run the phases **in order**. Each phase fully completes (audit → land all findings → re-verify) before the next begins.

---

### Phase 1 — spec/implementation drift audit

Compare the implementation against the authoritative spec (`spec/components/<category>/<component>.md` plus any foundation specs it transitively depends on) section-by-section. For every drift, propose the **best outcome independent of which side is currently right**. Sometimes the spec wins, sometimes the impl wins, sometimes the right answer is neither.

The canonical entry-prompt for this phase (use this wording when the user asks you to start the audit explicitly):

> "Now let's do an audit on how well our new code follows our spec. Do an in-depth check for spec drift and suggest recommendations for each finding. In those recommendations, disregard both the spec and the current implementation as defaults — the recommendation should be the best outcome possible. Sometimes that's the spec, sometimes the implementation, sometimes a mix, sometimes a totally different thing."

#### Precondition: both files must exist

Before reading anything, verify the touched implementation has a corresponding spec file at `spec/components/<category>/<name>.md` (or the relevant `spec/foundation/` path for foundation-layer changes). If the spec is missing, **stop the audit and surface this to the user** — an unspecced module is a planning failure that should be resolved (write the spec, or split the change) before auditing, not papered over by this skill. Likewise, if the implementation file referenced by the issue is missing or empty, surface that as a precondition failure rather than auditing against an empty diff.

#### Procedure

1. Read the spec file end-to-end (use `Read` with no offset/limit so you have the whole thing).
2. Read the implementation file end-to-end (same).
3. Walk every public surface declared in the spec — every struct, enum, function, method, derive list, default value, constant, public re-export. For each:
   - Does the spec define it? Does the impl match exactly?
   - Are there extras in the impl not in the spec? Are they justified?
   - Are there spec items the impl skipped? Are they critical?
   - **Does the spec's prose match the impl's actual behavior?** This is the silent-contract-violation class — words that mean something specific in the spec but were implemented as something close-but-different (e.g., "case folding" in the spec but `to_lowercase` in the impl).
4. For each finding, write up:
   - What the spec says, with line numbers
   - What the impl does, with line numbers
   - Why it drifted (often a tradeoff the implementer made under acceptance-criteria pressure)
   - **Best-outcome recommendation** — independent of which side is currently right
5. Group findings by severity (High / Medium / Low) and present as a table to the user.
6. Land all of them. Update the spec where the impl is right, update the impl where the spec is right, do both where neither was right, change *only* the spec wording (and add tests proving the new wording) when the contract was subtly wrong all along.

#### Special case: both the spec and the implementation are wrong

A small fraction of drift findings are *not* "one side is right, the other drifted" — both sides are incorrect, incomplete, or built on a flawed premise. This is rare but real (the task #204 eszett case is an example: the spec implied `ß ↔ ss` equivalence, the impl used `to_lowercase`, but neither matched the actual TR21 case-folding semantics needed). Handle this case differently from routine drift:

1. **Escalate to the user via `AskUserQuestion`** — don't unilaterally pick a "best outcome" when no precedent exists. A both-wrong case is a real design decision, not a routine drift fix; the user owns the call.
2. **Once the user picks a direction**, land *all three* together in this same PR: the new spec wording, the new implementation, and the tests proving the new design. Skipping any of the three leaves a different kind of drift behind.
3. **Write the test first** (TDD per CLAUDE.md), watch it fail under the old code, then change the impl, then update the spec to describe what the new tests assert.

#### Output table format

| Finding ID | Severity | What spec says | What impl does | Best outcome | Where to land |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| H1 | High | … | … | … | spec §X / impl `path:line` |

End with a disposition summary: *"N spec-only changes, M impl-only changes, K both, Q status-quo-was-correct."*

---

### Phase 2 — iterative "anything else missing?" passes

Once Phase 1 findings are landed, the next blind spot is the surface Phase 1 didn't look at: adapter specs, feature flags, prelude exports, framework wiring, foundation patterns that deserve promotion, catalog/manifest entries. This phase is **iterative** — keep asking "anything else?" until two consecutive rounds find nothing new.

The canonical entry-prompt (one per round):

> "Any other improvement that we might still be missing?"

#### Surfaces to actively check each round

This is a non-exhaustive checklist. Each round, walk through it explicitly:

- **Adapter specs** at `spec/leptos-components/<category>/<component>.md` and `spec/dioxus-components/<category>/<component>.md` — do they describe the new core surface accurately? Are their canonical implementation sketches up-to-date with the actual `Api` / function signatures? Do they reference the right helper methods (e.g., `Api::root_attrs()`) instead of hardcoded data attribute strings?
- **Adapter feature wiring** in `crates/ars-leptos/Cargo.toml` and `crates/ars-dioxus/Cargo.toml` — does the adapter's `icu4x` / `web-intl` feature need to enable any new `ars-components/<feature>` flag for the new code to be reachable?
- **Adapter `prelude.rs`** — does the new public API need to flow through? (Only if an adapter wrapper exists for the new component; if not, defer to the adapter task.)
- **Workspace `spec/manifest.toml`** and **`foundation/02-component-catalog.md`** — is the new component registered and statused? (For brand-new components only.)
- **Foundation specs** (`spec/foundation/00-*` through `spec/foundation/11-*`) — does the new code expose a missing shared abstraction worth promoting? CLAUDE.md `spec/CLAUDE.md` says: *"If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec."*
- **New crate dependencies** — was the user notified per CLAUDE.md's "Do not add a new dependency crate without explicit user approval first" rule? Did the implementation add `i18n`-style feature flags that warrant a CLAUDE.md note?
- **`.cargo/mutants.toml`** — are any new equivalent mutations documented with justifications? (Phase 3 will validate this; surface it here if a mutation that *should* be equivalent isn't documented yet.)

#### Termination

Stop when:

- Two consecutive rounds surface no new findings, **OR**
- Three rounds have been completed and the remaining findings are honestly out-of-scope (genuine workspace-wide refactors that would derail this PR).

Do **not** stop after one clean round — the second round nearly always surfaces items the first ignored because they felt out-of-scope. With the "no deferral" rule active, they're back in scope.

#### Output per round

A list of findings with the same "best outcome" framing as Phase 1, plus an explicit statement at the end: *"That's everything I can find this round. Want me to do another?"* — knowing the answer will be yes for at least one more round.

---

### Phase 3 — test coverage audit

By this point the code is convention-aligned and well-specced. Now check the **test surface** — both depth (line / branch / mutation coverage) and breadth (does every test type that *should* exist for this kind of change exist?).

The canonical entry-prompt:

> "How good is our test coverage here, by looking at the code paths, checking the coverage report and considering the many kinds of tests that we are intending to have (including snapshot tests, proptests, and e2e tests)? Can it be improved?"

#### Test types and what each catches

| Test type | What it catches | How to assess for the new code |
|---|---|---|
| **Unit tests** (inline `#[cfg(test)]`) | Logic + edge cases on the named acceptance criteria | List every public API + every edge case the spec describes; cross-reference with the test list |
| **Snapshot tests** (insta) | `AttrMap` / chunk-output shape regressions | Check coverage of every anatomy part and every output-affecting prop / state / context branch |
| **Property tests** (proptest) | Invariant violations across the input space | Check that invariants are non-trivial (roundtrip, idempotence, no-adjacent-X, etc.) and the input strategy is broad enough to actually exercise edge locales / multi-byte / large inputs |
| **Mutation tests** (`cargo xmutants`) | Tests that *exist* but don't actually constrain behavior | **Not run as part of this audit.** Owned by the Nightly CI `mutation-testing` job; `MISSED` mutants are triaged in a follow-up, never as a pre-PR gate. Use the coverage report + breadth audit to judge test quality here. |
| **Doc tests** | Public-API examples broken by future changes | Check `///` blocks for at least one `# Examples` block on the canonical entry point |
| **Spec-conformance tests** | Anatomy drift between spec and impl | Check `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/*.rs` for an entry covering the new component's `Part` enum and public API/attribute contract; this is mandatory for every new framework-agnostic component |
| **Code coverage** (cargo llvm-cov + xtask wasm path) | Unreachable / under-tested code; threshold drift | Use the right command for the crate (see "Procedure" below) — bare `cargo llvm-cov` does **not** measure adapter wasm code paths. Always finish with `cargo xtask coverage check-all --file <lcov>` to enforce the same thresholds CI does. |
| **E2E / browser tests** (wasm-bindgen-test) | DOM-level behavior in the adapter | N/A for agnostic-core changes. Required when the change touches `ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, or `ars-i18n` (`web-intl` path); these run via `cargo xtask test` or the adapter test-harness crates and execute in a browser via `wasm-bindgen-test`. Treat coverage from the wasm path (`cargo xtask coverage wasm`) as the source of truth for these — not the host-target llvm-cov number. |

#### Procedure

1. **Coverage report.** The command depends on which crate the change touches — this workspace measures host-target and wasm32 paths separately because adapter code (`ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, the `ars-i18n` `web-intl` path, the adapter test harnesses) has `target_arch = "wasm32"` / `feature = "web"` code that bare host-target `cargo llvm-cov` cannot reach.

   **For native-only crates** (`ars-components`, `ars-collections`, `ars-core`, `ars-forms`, `ars-interactions`, `ars-a11y`, `xtask`):
   ```bash
   cargo llvm-cov test -p <crate> --features <relevant> --lib --no-fail-fast --text -- <module>::
   ```

   **For adapter crates / `web-intl` paths** (`ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, `ars-test-harness-leptos`, `ars-test-harness-dioxus`, or `ars-i18n` when the change is in the `web-intl` feature gate):
   ```bash
   cargo xtask coverage wasm \
     --package <crate> \
     --feature <feat1> --feature <feat2> \
     --file wasm.lcov.info
   ```
   (`--feature` is singular and repeatable, NOT `--features` — it mirrors cargo's `--feature` convention; use `--no-default-features` to drop defaults.) This uses `wasm-bindgen-test`'s experimental coverage recipe with LLVM 22 / `clang-22` (the same recipe CI nightly runs via `cargo xtask ci coverage`). The bare `cargo llvm-cov` command will silently report `0.00%` for the wasm-gated code paths, which is *not* the same as having no coverage — it means *unmeasured*. Don't trust low numbers on adapter crates from the native path.

   **For a change that touches both native and wasm paths** (e.g., a new ars-i18n helper consumed from both backends, or a refactor in `ars-dom` that affects native callers):
   ```bash
   # Generate the native lcov (workspace, excluding wasm-only crates):
   cargo llvm-cov --workspace \
     --exclude ars-leptos --exclude ars-dioxus \
     --exclude ars-test-harness-leptos --exclude ars-test-harness-dioxus \
     --exclude ars-derive --exclude xtask \
     --lcov --output-path native.lcov.info

   # Generate the wasm lcov for each adapter target you touched:
   cargo xtask coverage wasm --package <wasm-package> --feature <feat> --file wasm.lcov.info

   # Merge without double-counting duplicate lines (each `--file` adds an input):
   cargo xtask coverage merge \
     --file native.lcov.info \
     --file wasm.lcov.info \
     --output merged.lcov.info

   # Enforce per-crate spec-defined thresholds (same gate CI runs):
   cargo xtask coverage check-all --file merged.lcov.info
   ```

   The per-crate thresholds (line% / branch%) are encoded in `xtask::coverage::default_thresholds()` and include the wasm targets. `cargo xtask coverage check-all` is the same gate CI enforces, so a green local run guarantees green CI on the coverage step.

   Read the per-line annotated output from the `--text` invocations. Every `^0` marker is an unhit branch — investigate each one. Categorize as: real test gap / equivalent defensive guard / unreachable dead code. **Expected non-gaps:** no-op callback closures in tests (e.g., `|_| {}`) are intentionally uncovered and not a real gap — CLAUDE.md calls this out explicitly.

   **Not measured by either path:** `ars-derive` (proc-macro, runs in the compiler — exercised indirectly via `crates/ars-core/tests/derive_contract.rs`). Don't expect coverage numbers here.

2. **Mutation testing — NOT a gate here.** Do **not** run `cargo xmutants` as part of this audit, and do not block handoff on a clean local mutation run. Mutation coverage is owned by the Nightly CI `mutation-testing` job; surviving (`MISSED`) mutants it reports are triaged in a dedicated follow-up (add a killing test, or add a justified `.cargo/mutants.toml` `exclude_re` for a true equivalent mutation), not during initial delivery. Lean on the coverage report (step 1) and the breadth audit (step 3) to judge test quality instead. (A voluntary local mutation pass is allowed but never required.)

3. **Test-type breadth audit.** Walk the table above. For each test type, ask: *"Does this kind of change need this kind of test, and do we have one?"* Examples:
   - New framework-agnostic component with an anatomy table → spec-conformance test required
   - New public function → doc test with `# Examples` required
   - New invariants on a computation pipeline → proptest invariants strongly recommended
   - New `AttrMap` helpers → snapshot tests for each branch required

4. **Cumulative assessment.** Present the before/after metrics in a table:

| Metric | Before this phase | After this phase |
|---|---|---|
| Lines covered | x% | y% |
| Mutations caught | x/total | y/total |
| Unit tests | N | M |
| Doc tests | N | M |
| Spec-conformance test | yes/no | yes/no |

#### Output

A list of gaps with concrete reproducers (specific test inputs that trigger the missed branch). For each gap, classify the disposition (real-gap-add-test / equivalent-mutation-skip / dead-code-remove). End with a clear *"land all"* and the verification commands to confirm.

#### Anti-pattern: chasing 100% line coverage

The goal is **not** "100% line coverage at any cost". The goal is *"strong, behavior-constraining tests with no unreachable defensive code, and every test type that should exist does exist"*. A 99% line-coverage report whose tests make positive-direction assertions is stronger than a 100% report full of assertion-free walks. If the last 1% of uncovered code is a genuinely unreachable defensive guard, leave it. (Mutation testing — the eventual measure of whether tests *constrain* behavior — is owned by Nightly CI, not this audit.)

---

## After the three phases

Run the full verification suite one final time before handing off to the user:

```bash
# 1. Unit + integration tests, both feature-on and feature-off variants
cargo test -p <crate> --features <relevant> --all-targets
cargo test -p <crate> --lib                       # without the optional features

# 2. Workspace clippy (must be -D warnings clean)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --exclude ars-i18n -- -D warnings

# 3. Backend matrices for any feature-gated code
cargo clippy -p ars-i18n --no-default-features --features std,icu4x --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -p ars-i18n --no-default-features --features std,web-intl --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. Docs build (0 new warnings on the changed crate)
cargo doc -p <crate> --no-deps --all-features

# 5. Spec validation + snippet check
cargo xtask spec validate
cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets

# 6. Snapshot orphan check
cargo insta test --workspace --features <relevant> --unreferenced=reject

# 7. (Mutation testing is NOT run here — it is Nightly-CI-driven.)

# 8. Coverage thresholds — uses the same gate CI enforces. The recipe
#    depends on which crate the change touches: native-only changes can
#    use `cargo llvm-cov ... --lcov`; changes that touch adapter code or
#    `web-intl` paths require `cargo xtask coverage wasm` + `merge` first
#    (see Phase 3 above). Then:
cargo xtask coverage check-all --file lcov.info

# 9. Adapter builds reachable with feature wiring
cargo build -p ars-leptos --features icu4x
cargo build -p ars-dioxus --features icu4x,web
```

Then write **one consolidated user summary** covering all three phases' findings + the verification table. Hand off for user review per CLAUDE.md workflow step 8 (which was the old step 7 before this skill was inserted).

After the user approves commit/push and the PR is opened, **read and follow**
`.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` through to Codex 👍. That
skill is AGENTS.md step 12 — posting `@codex review` is only the trigger inside
the loop, not a substitute for the full poll/triage/fix cycle.

## Anti-patterns

- **Deferring** any finding ("we can fix that in a follow-up"). This is the rule the user set explicitly. Breaking it makes the audit ceremonial.
- **Loading the user with options mid-phase.** Make a clear best-outcome recommendation. Only escalate to `AskUserQuestion` when there's a genuine judgment call the user must make (e.g., behavior choices with real tradeoffs, not implementation details).
- **Skipping rounds of Phase 2** because the first round was clean. The second round nearly always finds something the first ignored.
- **Treating coverage % as the goal.** The goal is behavior-constraining tests and breadth-complete coverage, not a number.
- **Inventing cosmetic edits** the audits didn't surface. If a phase doesn't find a real finding, don't fabricate one — say "this round found nothing" and proceed.
- **Re-running tests after every micro-edit** during a phase. Land a batch of related findings, then verify, then move on.
- **Forgetting to update the named-tests file** when a new test exposes a real bug fixed in the same phase. The fix and the test land together.

## What this skill is not

- A code-review skill. Code review (style, naming, simplification) belongs to `pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer` and runs after the PR is opened.
- A pre-implementation skill. Before implementing, `feature-dev:feature-dev` and `superpowers:writing-plans` handle planning.
- A way to revisit acceptance criteria. The acceptance criteria are fixed by the GitHub issue. This skill assumes they're already met.

## Worked example — task #204

The flow this skill encodes was validated on GitHub issue #204 (`task: Implement Highlight agnostic core`). The initial implementation passed all 8 named acceptance-criterion tests on first try. Then:

- **Phase 1** surfaced 16 drift findings, including a silent contract violation (the spec said *"Unicode case folding"* but the impl used `to_lowercase` — they aren't equivalent: under `to_lowercase`, query `"ss"` does not match text `"ß"`, violating the spec's explicit eszett contract). Fix: add `ars_i18n::case_fold`, switch the matching pipeline, rewrite the German test to assert the actual three-direction equivalence.
- **Phase 2 round 1** surfaced 4 more items (adapter spec alignment, adapter feature wiring, roundtrip invariant test, proptest invariants).
- **Phase 2 round 2** surfaced 3 more (a `case_fold` vs `to_lowercase` semantic gap, foundation/10 parametric-anatomy concept, `cargo xmutants` scout).
- **Phase 3** surfaced 4 coverage gaps (dead `continue` branch, `merge_ranges` else branch, fold-expansion partial-match defense, spec-conformance test). Pushed line coverage from 98.67% to 99.91% with all 57 mutations caught.

The cumulative effect: PR landed with one user review pass instead of three, and the spec contract (`ß ↔ ss`) is actually true instead of cosmetically true. That's the value this skill creates.

13	fn calendar_date_default_for_part_all() -> CalendarDate {
14	    // Mirrors the `#[part(default = ...)]` value on `calendar::Part::Cell`
15	    // and `::CellTrigger`. `Part::all()` constructs these variants with the
16	    // declared defaults, and the spec-conformance helper compares
17	    // `Part::all()[i]` against the supplied expected parts via `PartialEq`,
18	    // so the test must use the same date the derive emits.
19	    CalendarDate::new_gregorian(1, 1, 1).expect("0001-01-01 is a valid Gregorian date")
20	}

lines: +// Props builders + Debug/PartialEq + prop sync via the live service

+    // The buffered "5" commits when the timer fires.
+    // "4" buffers (could be 40-49); FocusNext commits it then advances.
+    // The announcement includes seconds and the AM/PM suffix.
+    // de-DE is H23 (24-hour, DD.MM.YYYY) → no "AM/PM", "." date separator.
